Licensee shall be responsible for providing adequate test data, test scripts, and test resources for Acceptance Testing.

**Section 5.3 Acceptance Testing.**

> \(a\) **Acceptance Testing Period.** Following Licensor\'s delivery of a Deployment Notice with respect to each module or Deliverable, Licensee shall have thirty (30) calendar days (the \"**Acceptance Testing Period**\") to conduct Acceptance Testing to determine whether such module or Deliverable performs substantially in accordance with the applicable specifications set forth in the Documentation and the applicable Statement of Work.
>
> \(b\) **Acceptance Criteria.** The acceptance criteria for each module or Deliverable shall be as set forth in the applicable Statement of Work. In the absence of specific acceptance criteria in the Statement of Work, the acceptance criteria shall be whether the module or Deliverable performs substantially in accordance with the Documentation.
>
> \(c\) **Notice of Material Nonconformity.** If Licensee determines during the Acceptance Testing Period that the module or Deliverable contains a Material Nonconformity, Licensee shall deliver written notice to Licensor (a \"**Nonconformity Notice**\") prior to the expiration of the Acceptance Testing Period. Each Nonconformity Notice shall describe in reasonable detail the nature of the Material Nonconformity and the specific acceptance criteria that have not been met.
>
> \(d\) **Deemed Acceptance.** If Licensee fails to deliver a Nonconformity Notice to Licensor within the Acceptance Testing Period, the applicable module or Deliverable shall be deemed accepted by Licensee (\"**Acceptance**\"). For the avoidance of doubt, Licensee\'s use of any module or Deliverable in its production environment for business purposes (other than Acceptance Testing) during the Acceptance Testing Period shall constitute Acceptance of such module or Deliverable.
>
> \(e\) **Cure Period.** Upon receipt of a Nonconformity Notice, Licensor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to correct the Material Nonconformity within thirty (30) calendar days (the \"**Cure Period**\"). Upon completion of the correction, Licensor shall deliver a revised Deployment Notice, and Licensee shall have an additional fifteen (15) calendar day period to re-test the corrected module or Deliverable. If Licensee does not deliver a subsequent Nonconformity Notice within such fifteen (15) calendar day period, the module or Deliverable shall be deemed accepted.
>
> \(f\) **Failure to Cure.** If Licensor is unable to correct a Material Nonconformity within two (2) successive Cure Periods (totaling sixty (60) calendar days), Licensee\'s sole and exclusive remedy shall be to: (i) accept the module or Deliverable with the nonconformity and receive a mutually agreed-upon credit against future Fees; or (ii) reject the module or Deliverable and receive a pro-rata refund of any pre-paid Fees attributable to such module or Deliverable, calculated from the date of rejection through the end of the then-current License Term.
>
> \(g\) **Partial Acceptance.** Acceptance of one module or Deliverable shall not constitute acceptance of any other module or Deliverable.

**Section 5.4 Change Orders.**

> \(a\) **Change Order Process.** Either Party may request changes to the scope, schedule, or deliverables of any Statement of Work by submitting a written change order request (a \"**Change Order Request**\") to the other Party. Each Change Order Request shall describe in reasonable detail: (i) the proposed change; (ii) the reason for the change; (iii) the estimated impact on schedule, scope, and Fees; and (iv) any dependencies or prerequisites.

Each executed Change Order shall be incorporated into and governed by this Agreement and the applicable Statement of Work.

> \(d\) **Continued Performance.** Pending agreement on a Change Order, both Parties shall continue to perform their respective obligations under the existing Statement of Work without modification.

**[EXHIBIT A --- SOFTWARE DESCRIPTION AND SPECIFICATIONS]{.underline}**

**A.1 Licensed Software.** The Licensed Software licensed under this Agreement is the **NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module**, a component of Licensor\'s NexusCore cloud-based enterprise resource planning and data analytics platform. The Healthcare Analytics Module provides the following core functional areas:

> \(a\) **Clinical Analytics.** Real-time and retrospective analysis of clinical data, including patient outcomes, treatment patterns, readmission rates, length of stay metrics, and clinical quality measures. Supports integration with electronic health record (\"EHR\") systems through Licensor\'s standard API framework.
>
> \(b\) **Financial Analytics.** Revenue cycle analytics, cost analysis, payer mix analysis, denials management analytics, and financial forecasting capabilities. Supports integration with hospital billing systems and practice management platforms.
>
> \(c\) **Operational Analytics.** Operational performance dashboards, capacity utilization analysis, staffing analytics, supply chain analytics, and workflow optimization tools.
>
> \(d\) **Population Health Analytics.** Population health management analytics, including risk stratification, care gap identification, chronic disease management analytics, and preventive care tracking.
>
> \(e\) **Regulatory Compliance Analytics.** Analytics and reporting tools designed to support compliance with healthcare regulatory requirements, including CMS quality reporting programs, HEDIS measures, and state-specific reporting requirements.
>
> \(f\) **Reporting and Visualization.** Configurable dashboards, ad-hoc reporting capabilities, scheduled report generation, data visualization tools, and export capabilities in multiple formats (PDF, Excel, CSV, HL7 FHIR).

**A.2 System Architecture.** The Licensed Software operates as a multi-tenant, cloud-hosted software-as-a-service application. The Licensed Software is hosted on enterprise-grade cloud infrastructure managed by Licensor or its designated hosting provider. Key architectural features include:

> \(a\) multi-tenant architecture with logical data separation;
>
> \(b\) role-based access control and authentication (including SAML 2.0 and OAuth 2.0 support);
>
> \(c\) encryption of data in transit (TLS 1.2 or higher) and at rest (AES-256);
>
> \(d\) high-availability architecture with geographic redundancy;
>
> \(e\) RESTful API framework for system integration;
>
> \(f\) configurable ETL (extract, transform, load) pipelines for data ingestion; and
>
> \(g\) scalable processing engine for real-time and batch analytics.

**A.3 Supported Integrations.** The Licensed Software is designed to integrate with major EHR platforms through Licensor\'s standard API framework, subject to proper configuration and the provision of accurate integration specifications by Licensee. Licensor maintains pre-built connectors for the following EHR platforms as of the Effective Date: Epic, Cerner (Oracle Health), MEDITECH, Allscripts, athenahealth, and eClinicalWorks. Integration with other EHR platforms may require custom API development pursuant to a Change Order.

**A.4 Documentation.** Licensor shall provide the following Documentation in connection with the Licensed Software:

> \(a\) Administrator Guide;
>
> \(b\) End-User Guide;
>
> \(c\) API Integration Guide and Technical Reference;
>
> \(d\) Data Dictionary and Schema Documentation;
>
> \(e\) Configuration Guide;
>
> \(f\) Release Notes (for each update, patch, and upgrade); and
>
> \(g\) Online Help System accessible within the Licensed Software.

**A.5 Technical Requirements.** Licensee is responsible for ensuring that its end-user systems and network environment meet the minimum technical requirements specified in the Documentation, including: (a) supported web browsers and versions; (b) minimum internet bandwidth requirements; and (c) network configuration requirements for API integrations. Licensor shall not be responsible for performance issues arising from Licensee\'s failure to meet such technical requirements.

**[EXHIBIT B --- SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**B.1 Uptime Commitment.** Licensor commits to a monthly uptime percentage of ninety-nine and seven-tenths percent (99.7%) for the Licensed Software, measured on a calendar-month basis, excluding Scheduled Maintenance Windows and Force Majeure Events (the \"**Uptime Commitment**\"). \"**Uptime Percentage**\" is calculated as: ((Total Minutes in Calendar Month -- Downtime Minutes) / Total Minutes in Calendar Month) x 100.

**B.2 Scheduled Maintenance Windows.** Licensor\'s standard Scheduled Maintenance Window is Sunday, 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM Central Time. Licensor shall provide at least seventy-two (72) hours\' prior notice of Scheduled Maintenance that may result in downtime exceeding thirty (30) minutes. Total Scheduled Maintenance downtime shall not exceed eight (8) hours per calendar month.

**B.3 Severity Classifications and Response Times.**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Severity Level**        **Description**                                                                      **Initial Response Time**   **Target Resolution Time**
  ------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --------------------------- ----------------------------
  Severity 1 --- Critical   Production system down or major functionality unavailable; no workaround available   1 hour (24/7)               4 hours

  Severity 2 --- High       Major functionality impaired; workaround available but degraded performance          4 hours (business hours)    1 Business Day

  Severity 3 --- Medium     Minor functionality impaired; workaround available with minimal impact               8 business hours            5 Business Days

  Severity 4 --- Low        Cosmetic issue, documentation error, or enhancement request                          2 Business Days             Next scheduled release
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**B.4 Escalation Procedures.**

> \(a\) **Level 1 --- Help Desk.** Initial support contact via telephone, email, or online ticketing system. Available Monday through Friday, 7:00 AM to 9:00 PM Central Time, and Saturday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Central Time (excluding Licensor-observed holidays).
>
> \(b\) **Level 2 --- Technical Support Engineers.** Escalation for issues not resolved at Level 1 within the applicable response time. Available during business hours.
>
> \(c\) **Level 3 --- Engineering Team.** Escalation for complex issues requiring development-level investigation or code changes. Coordinated by Licensor\'s assigned Technical Account Manager.
>
> \(d\) **Level 4 --- Executive Escalation.** For Severity 1 issues not resolved within the target resolution time, escalation to Licensor\'s VP of Customer Success and, if necessary, to the Licensor Project Manager and Steering Committee.

**B.5 Service Level Credits.**

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Monthly Uptime Percentage**       **Service Level Credit (% of Monthly Maintenance & Support Fee)**
  ----------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------
  99.0% to \< 99.7%                   5%

  98.0% to \< 99.0%                   10%

  95.0% to \< 98.0%                   20%

  Below 95.0%                         30%
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Service level credits shall be applied as a credit against Licensee\'s next invoice. In no event shall service level credits in any calendar month exceed thirty percent (30%) of the Monthly Maintenance and Support Fee for such month.
