    caption(doc,
            "DEFENDANT MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS "
            "PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO FEDERAL RULE OF "
            "CIVIL PROCEDURE 12(b)(6)",
            response_note="Response Due: Twenty-one (21) days after filing of this Motion")

    # ---- Certificate of Conference ----
    heading(doc, "CERTIFICATE OF CONFERENCE", level=1, space_before=0)
    para(doc,
         "Pursuant to Section 3.1 of the Court's Standing Order for Civil Cases and Local "
         "Rule CV-7, counsel for Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. (\"Meridian\") "
         "conferred in good faith with counsel for Plaintiff Arcadia Health Systems, LLC "
         "(\"Arcadia\") regarding the relief sought in this Motion. The parties discussed "
         "the legal grounds raised herein, including the statutory exemption applicable to "
         "Arcadia's DTPA claim, the bar on unjust enrichment in the presence of an express "
         "contract, and the contractual and pleading deficiencies affecting Arcadia's tort "
         "and contract claims. Arcadia declined to voluntarily dismiss or narrow any claim. "
         "Because this Motion presents legal questions on which no compromise is possible, "
         "Meridian submits that further conferral would be futile and respectfully files "
         "this Motion.")

    # ---- Introduction ----
    heading(doc, "I.  INTRODUCTION", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "This is a breach-of-contract case dressed up as something more. Arcadia Health "
         "Systems, LLC\u2014a sophisticated commercial entity with approximately $62 million in "
         "annual revenue, represented by experienced outside counsel throughout an "
         "arm's-length negotiation\u2014paid $14.7 million (plus $2.35 million in mutually "
         "executed Change Orders) to license and implement Meridian's NexusCore healthcare "
         "analytics platform. Dissatisfied with the result, Arcadia now seeks $47.3 million "
         "by repackaging a single commercial dispute into five causes of action and asking "
         "the Court to disregard the comprehensive risk-allocation framework that Arcadia's "
         "own counsel negotiated and Arcadia knowingly accepted.")
    para(doc,
         "The First Amended Complaint (\"FAC\") cannot survive scrutiny under Bell Atlantic "
         "Corp. v. Twombly, 550 U.S. 544 (2007), and Ashcroft v. Iqbal, 556 U.S. 662 (2009). "
         "Several counts fail on the face of the pleadings and the governing contract as a "
         "matter of law, requiring no factual development:")
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Count V (DTPA) is barred by the statutory exemption in Texas Business & "
         "Commerce Code \u00a7 17.49(f), because the transaction exceeds $500,000 in total "
         "consideration and Arcadia is not an individual.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Count IV (Unjust Enrichment) is barred because a valid, express contract\u2014"
         "the MSLSA\u2014governs the same subject matter.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Counts II and III (Fraud and Negligent Misrepresentation) fail for multiple "
         "independent reasons: the alleged statements are non-actionable puffery; the claims "
         "are barred by the economic loss rule; the MSLSA's integration and non-reliance "
         "clause precludes reliance on extra-contractual representations; Arcadia cannot "
         "establish justifiable reliance; and the fraud claim does not satisfy Rule 9(b).",
         indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Count I (Breach of Contract) is barred or must be radically limited by the "
         "MSLSA's deemed-acceptance provision, expired limited warranty, exclusive-remedy "
         "clause, consequential-damages waiver, and aggregate-liability cap\u2014all of which "
         "Arcadia negotiated and accepted.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "Meridian therefore moves, pursuant to Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 12(b)(6), to "
         "dismiss all five counts of the FAC with prejudice, or, in the alternative, to "
         "dismiss all damages barred by the MSLSA's limitation-of-liability provisions.")

    # ---- Factual Background ----
    heading(doc, "II.  FACTUAL BACKGROUND", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "A court may consider documents referenced in the complaint and central to the "
         "plaintiff's claims without converting a Rule 12(b)(6) motion into one for summary "
         "judgment. Lormand v. US Unwired, Inc., 565 F.3d 228, 255\u201356 (5th Cir. 2009). "
         "The FAC repeatedly references and relies on the Master Software License and "
         "Services Agreement (\"MSLSA\"), Statement of Work #1 (\"SOW-1\"), the four Change "
         "Orders, Meridian's December 9, 2021 written proposal, the August 25, 2022 project "
         "status report, and Dr. Okonkwo's March 8, 2023 email. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 27, 33\u201335, "
         "44, 46, 50. Those documents, together with the FAC's own allegations, establish "
         "the following.")

    heading(doc, "A.  The MSLSA Is a Fully Negotiated, Comprehensive Risk-Allocation Agreement.",
            level=2)
    para(doc,
         "On March 15, 2022, two sophisticated commercial entities executed the MSLSA and "
         "SOW-1 for the licensing and implementation of NexusCore. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 33\u201335. "
         "Arcadia is a Texas limited liability company with approximately $62 million in "
         "annual revenue. FAC \u00b6 6. Meridian is a publicly traded Delaware corporation. "
         "FAC \u00b6 9. The FAC itself acknowledges that Arcadia \"engaged outside counsel to "
         "assist in the negotiation process\" and that Meridian \"insisted on its standard "
         "contractual terms.\" FAC \u00b6 32. The MSLSA reflects the result of that negotiation "
         "and contains a deliberate, comprehensive allocation of risk:")
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Integration and non-reliance clause (\u00a7 12.1): the MSLSA \"constitutes the "
         "entire agreement between the Parties\" and \"supersedes all prior and contemporaneous "
         "agreements, proposals, representations, and understandings, whether oral or "
         "written,\" and \"Each Party acknowledges that, in entering into this Agreement, it "
         "has not relied upon any statement, representation, warranty, or agreement of the "
         "other Party except as expressly set forth in this Agreement.\"", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Aggregate-liability cap (\u00a7 8.1): each party's aggregate liability \"shall "
         "not exceed the total fees paid or payable by Licensee during the twelve (12) month "
         "period immediately preceding the event giving rise to such liability,\" regardless "
         "of the form of action.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Consequential-damages waiver (\u00a7 8.2): neither party is liable for "
         "\"indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive, or exemplary damages, "
         "including \u2026 damages for loss of profits, goodwill, use, data, or other intangible "
         "losses.\"", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Exclusive remedies (\u00a7 8.3): the MSLSA's remedies are the licensee's \"sole "
         "and exclusive remedies for any breach.\"", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Limited warranty and disclaimer (\u00a7\u00a7 9.1, 9.4): a 90-day warranty that "
         "the software will \"perform substantially in accordance with the Documentation,\" "
         "with repair/replacement or a pro-rata refund as the sole remedy; all other "
         "warranties, express or implied, are disclaimed.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Deemed acceptance (\u00a7 5.3): a 30-day acceptance-testing window following "
         "deployment, with acceptance \"deemed\" if the licensee fails to deliver written "
         "notice of material nonconformity.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Governing law (\u00a7 12.7): Delaware law, without regard to conflict-of-law "
         "principles.", indent=0.25)

    heading(doc, "B.  Data Migration Was Solely Arcadia's Responsibility.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The FAC's central narrative\u2014that Meridian \"failed to properly plan and execute "
         "the data migration,\" FAC \u00b6 43\u2014is foreclosed by the contract. Section 3.2 of "
         "SOW-1 provides that Arcadia \"shall be solely responsible for all data migration "
         "activities, including extraction, transformation, and loading of legacy data into "
         "NexusCore,\" and that Meridian's role was limited to \"advisory consulting services\" "
         "with \"no responsibility for the accuracy, completeness, or integrity of migrated "
         "data.\" Exhibit C to the MSLSA reiterates that Meridian \"shall have no "
         "responsibility or liability\" for data migrated by Arcadia or its contractors. The "
         "FAC nonetheless attributes to Meridian the failures of Arcadia's own independently "
         "retained systems integrator, Linden Park Consulting, LLC. See FAC \u00b6\u00b6 43, 47, "
         "72(d).")

    heading(doc, "C.  Arcadia Conducted Independent Due Diligence and Accepted the Risk "
                 "Allocation Knowingly.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The FAC admits that Arcadia's CTO, Martin Schreiber, \"conducted certain independent "
         "due diligence, including reference calls with two existing Meridian healthcare "
         "clients\" before contract execution. FAC \u00b6 31. The FAC further admits that "
         "Arcadia \"engaged outside counsel to assist in the negotiation process\" and "
         "attempted (unsuccessfully) to negotiate \"enhanced limitation of liability "
         "provisions and expanded warranty protections.\" FAC \u00b6 32. And the FAC concedes "
         "that Meridian's December 9, 2021 written proposal \"contained certain general "
         "disclaimers.\" FAC \u00b6 27. Those disclaimers expressly stated that \"[e]stimated "
         "timelines and performance metrics are provided for planning purposes only and do "
         "not constitute guarantees,\" that \"Meridian does not warrant that integration with "
         "any particular EHR system will be seamless, error-free, or achievable within any "
         "specific timeframe,\" and that Arcadia \"should not rely solely on the information "
         "in this proposal.\" These admissions defeat any claim of justifiable reliance on "
         "pre-contractual sales statements.")

    heading(doc, "D.  Arcadia Deemed-Accepted the Software and Its Warranty Has Expired.",
            level=2)
    para(doc,
         "NexusCore achieved Go-Live on January 15, 2023. FAC \u00b6 45. Under \u00a7 5.3, Arcadia "
         "had 30 days\u2014until February 14, 2023\u2014to deliver written notice of any material "
         "nonconformity. Arcadia did not do so. Its first written communication about "
         "performance\u2014Dr. Okonkwo's March 8, 2023 email\u2014was sent 22 days after the "
         "acceptance window closed and reported only \"intermittent latency issues\" and "
         "\"occasional report generation errors.\" FAC \u00b6 50. Acceptance was therefore "
         "deemed granted as of February 14, 2023. The \u00a7 9.1 limited warranty ran for 90 "
         "days from Go-Live and expired on April 15, 2023, with repair/replacement or a "
         "pro-rata refund as the sole remedy.")

    # ---- Legal Standard ----
    heading(doc, "III.  LEGAL STANDARD", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "To survive a Rule 12(b)(6) motion, a complaint must contain sufficient factual "
         "matter to \"state a claim to relief that is plausible on its face.\" Twombly, 550 "
         "U.S. at 570; Iqbal, 556 U.S. at 678. The Court \"accept[s] all well-pleaded facts "
         "as true,\" but need not \"accept as true \u2026 legal conclusions couched as factual "
         "allegations.\" Lormand, 565 F.3d at 255\u201356. \"Threadbare recitals of the elements "
         "of a cause of action, supported by mere conclusory statements, do not suffice.\" "
         "Iqbal, 556 U.S. at 678. As this Court's Standing Order confirms, claims sounding in "
         "fraud must satisfy Rule 9(b)'s particularity requirement, including the \"who, "
         "what, when, where, and how\" of the alleged fraud. Standing Order \u00a7 4.2; "
         "Benchmark Elecs., Inc. v. J.M. Huber Corp., 343 F.3d 719, 724 (5th Cir. 2003).")
    para(doc,
         "Meridian preserved its Rule 12(b)(6) defenses in its Answer, including the First "
         "Affirmative Defense (failure to state a claim) and an express reservation of the "
         "right to move under Rule 12(b)(6). This Motion is therefore timely under the "
         "Court's Standing Order \u00a7 3.4 and the Scheduling Order's January 15, 2024 "
         "dispositive-motion deadline. The Court may consider the MSLSA, SOW-1, and Change "
         "Orders, which are referenced in and central to the FAC. Lormand, 565 F.3d at "
         "255\u201356.")

    # ---- Argument ----
    heading(doc, "IV.  ARGUMENT", level=1)

    # Count V
    heading(doc, "A.  Count V (DTPA) Must Be Dismissed Under the \u00a7 17.49(f) Statutory "
                 "Exemption.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Count V fails as a matter of law on the face of the pleadings. Section 17.49(f) of "
         "the Texas Business & Commerce Code exempts from DTPA coverage any \"cause of action "
         "arising from a transaction, a project, or a set of transactions \u2026 relating to the "
         "same project, involving total consideration by the consumer of more than $500,000,\" "
         "unless the consumer is an individual. Tex. Bus. & Com. Code \u00a7 17.49(f). Both "
         "conditions are satisfied here, and both are established by the FAC's own "
         "allegations.")
    para(doc,
         "First, Arcadia is not an individual. The FAC alleges that Arcadia is a Texas "
         "limited liability company. FAC \u00b6 6. Second, the total consideration vastly "
         "exceeds $500,000. The FAC alleges a base contract value of $14,700,000, FAC "
         "\u00b6\u00b6 34, 69, plus four Change Orders totaling $2,350,000, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 46\u201347, "
         "100\u2014a total of $17,050,000, more than thirty-four times the statutory threshold. "
         "The exemption is determined by the total consideration involved in the transaction, "
         "not by the amount of damages claimed. See PPG Indus., Inc. v. JMB/Houston Ctrs. "
         "Partners Ltd., 146 S.W.3d 79, 89 (Tex. App.\u2014Houston [1st Dist.] 2004, no pet.). "
         "Because the \u00a7 17.49(f) exemption is dispositive and turns solely on facts alleged "
         "in the FAC and a statute of which the Court may take judicial notice, Count V must "
         "be dismissed.")
    para(doc,
         "Count V fails for additional, independent reasons. Arcadia is not a \"consumer\" "
         "within the DTPA's meaning: this is a sophisticated, $14.7 million enterprise-"
         "software transaction negotiated at arm's length between two commercial entities, "
         "each represented by counsel\u2014not the consumer transaction the DTPA was enacted to "
         "protect. See Riverside Nat'l Bank v. Lewis, 603 S.W.2d 169, 174\u201375 (Tex. 1980). "
         "Moreover, the alleged misrepresentations are non-actionable puffery (see Section "
         "IV.C.3, below), and Arcadia cannot establish \"producing cause\" because the "
         "implementation delays and data-quality issues it complains of were caused by its "
         "own failures and those of its systems integrator, not by any sales statement. See "
         "Melody Home Mfg. Co. v. Barnes, 741 S.W.2d 349, 351\u201352 (Tex. 1987).")

    # Count IV
    heading(doc, "B.  Count IV (Unjust Enrichment) Must Be Dismissed Because an Express "
                 "Contract Governs the Same Subject Matter.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Under both Texas and Delaware law, a claim for unjust enrichment (or quantum "
         "meruit) is unavailable where a valid, express contract governs the subject matter "
         "of the dispute. Fortune Prod. Co. v. Conoco, Inc., 52 S.W.3d 671, 684 (Tex. 2000); "
         "Nemec v. Shrader, 991 A.2d 1120, 1130 (Del. 2010). The \"same subject matter\" test "
         "is applied broadly: if the contract addresses the general subject area of the "
         "dispute, unjust enrichment is barred. Excess Underwriters at Lloyd's v. Frank's "
         "Casing Crew & Rental Tools, Inc., 246 S.W.3d 42, 59\u201360 (Tex. 2008). Critically, "
         "the fact that contractual limitations (such as a liability cap or consequential-"
         "damages waiver) may limit recovery under the contract does not create a \"gap\" "
         "that unjust enrichment can fill. Id. at 59. \"Equity follows the law\"; where "
         "parties have allocated risk through contract, equity will not rewrite the bargain.")
    para(doc,
         "Count IV is irreconcilably inconsistent with Count I. In Count I, Arcadia alleges "
         "that a valid, enforceable MSLSA exists and that Meridian breached it. FAC \u00b6 69. "
         "Arcadia does not contend that the MSLSA is void or unenforceable\u2014to the contrary, "
         "it relies on the MSLSA as the foundation of its breach-of-contract claim. Count IV "
         "is pleaded only \"in the alternative \u2026 to the extent the MSLSA or any provision "
         "thereof is found to be unenforceable.\" FAC \u00b6 99. But Arcadia never alleges any "
         "ground of unenforceability. The MSLSA indisputably covers the entire scope of the "
         "parties' software-licensing and implementation relationship\u2014the very payments "
         "Arcadia seeks to recover as restitution. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 100\u2013103. Under Fortune "
         "Production and Excess Underwriters, unjust enrichment is unavailable as a matter of "
         "law, and Count IV must be dismissed.")

    # Count II
    heading(doc, "C.  Count II (Fraud) Must Be Dismissed on Four Independent Grounds.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MSLSA's \u00a7 12.7 designates Delaware law to govern the contract. To the extent "
         "Texas law governs the tort claims under the most-significant-relationship test, the "
         "fraud claim fails under either body of law, for the reasons below.")

    heading(doc, "1.  The FAC does not satisfy Rule 9(b).", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Fraud must be pleaded with particularity, specifying \"the statements contended to "
         "be fraudulent, identify[ing] the speaker, stat[ing] when and where the statements "
         "were made, and explain[ing] why the statements were fraudulent.\" Benchmark "
         "Elecs., 343 F.3d at 724. While the FAC identifies James Poletti and approximate "
         "dates, it does not plead why the statements were false when made. Its theory is "
         "entirely post-hoc: because NexusCore allegedly performed poorly after Go-Live, the "
         "pre-sale statements must have been false. That reasoning is insufficient. See "
         "Flaherty & Crumrine Preferred Income Fund, Inc. v. TXU Corp., 565 F.3d 200, 207\u201308 "
         "(5th Cir. 2009) (post-hoc product failure does not support an inference of "
         "scienter absent internal documents, contemporaneous admissions, or motive). The "
         "FAC's scienter allegations\u2014that Meridian \"knew or should have known\" the "
         "statements were false, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 30, 81\u2014are precisely the conclusory averments "
         "Rule 9(b) forbids. The concealment allegations (\u00b6\u00b6 56\u201360) are pleaded "
         "\"on information and belief\" without any factual basis, which this Court's "
         "Standing Order \u00a7 4.2 holds insufficient.")

    heading(doc, "2.  The economic loss rule bars the fraud claim.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Under Delaware law, \"where a plaintiff's claims arise solely from the contractual "
         "relationship between the parties, and the damages sought are exclusively economic "
         "losses \u2026 the economic loss doctrine bars recovery in tort.\" Brasby v. Morris "
         "Dynamics, Inc., 947 A.2d 1042, 1049 (Del. 2008). The doctrine applies with "
         "particular force where sophisticated parties represented by counsel negotiated a "
         "comprehensive written agreement containing an integration clause and warranty "
         "disclaimers. Kuhn Constr., Inc. v. Diamond State Port Corp., 990 A.2d 393, 401\u201302 "
         "(Del. 2010). Under Texas law, the rule bars tort recovery where (1) the losses are "
         "purely economic, (2) they relate to the subject matter of a contract, and (3) the "
         "defendant owes no duty independent of the contract. Chapman Custom Homes, Inc. v. "
         "Dallas Plumbing Co., 445 S.W.3d 716, 718\u201319 (Tex. 2014); Sharyland Water Supply "
         "Corp. v. City of Alton, 354 S.W.3d 407, 415\u201316 (Tex. 2011).")
    para(doc,
         "All three conditions are met. Arcadia's damages\u2014$14.7 million in contract value, "
         "$18.4 million in lost profits, $6.7 million in operating costs, and $5 million in "
         "reputational harm, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 61\u201366\u2014are purely economic. They all relate to "
         "NexusCore, the subject of the MSLSA. And Meridian owes no duty to Arcadia "
         "independent of the MSLSA: there is no fiduciary relationship, no professional duty, "
         "and no statutory duty beyond the contract. The alleged misrepresentations concern "
         "the very quality and performance of NexusCore that the MSLSA addresses through its "
         "warranty (\u00a7 9.1), disclaimer (\u00a7 9.4), and integration clause (\u00a7 12.1). "
         "Arcadia's fraud claim is, at bottom, a claim that Meridian did not deliver software "
         "that performed as promised\u2014\"the very essence of a breach of contract claim.\" "
         "Sharyland, 354 S.W.3d at 415. That repackaging is barred.")

    heading(doc, "3.  The alleged statements are non-actionable puffery.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "The Fifth Circuit holds that \"a general claim of superiority that is so vague that "
         "it can be understood only as the seller's opinion\" is non-actionable puffery. "
         "Pizza Hut, Inc. v. Papa John's Int'l, Inc., 227 F.3d 489, 497 (5th Cir. 2000); see "
         "also Castrol Inc. v. Pennzoil Co., 987 F.2d 939, 945 (3d Cir. 1993). "
         "Forward-looking statements about a product's expected performance, made in a sales "
         "context, are likewise puffery upon which a sophisticated buyer cannot reasonably "
         "rely. Presidio Enters., Inc. v. Warner Bros. Distributing Corp., 784 F.2d 674, "
         "679\u201380 (5th Cir. 1986). The FAC's lead examples are textbook puffery: \"industry-"
         "leading performance for healthcare analytics\" and \"30-40% improvement in "
         "reporting efficiency\" are vague, subjective claims of superiority and generalized "
         "projections. FAC \u00b6 23. The \"seamless integration\" statement, FAC \u00b6 25, is "
         "directly contradicted by Meridian's December 9, 2021 proposal, which expressly "
         "disclaimed that integration would be \"seamless, error-free, or achievable within "
         "any specific timeframe.\" FAC \u00b6 27. Puffery remains non-actionable even when "
         "embedded in a sales presentation. Pizza Hut, 227 F.3d at 498\u201399.")

    heading(doc, "4.  The integration and non-reliance clause bars reliance, and reliance "
                 "was not justifiable.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Section 12.1 is both a comprehensive integration clause and an express non-reliance "
         "disclaimer: the parties acknowledged that they \"ha[ve] not relied upon any "
         "statement, representation, warranty, or agreement of the other Party except as "
         "expressly set forth in this Agreement.\" Under Delaware law, \"a party to a "
         "contract cannot promise, in a clear integration clause of a negotiated agreement, "
         "that it is not relying on promises or representations made by the other party "
         "outside of the agreement, and then assert a claim for fraud based on those very "
         "representations.\" Eagle Indus., Inc. v. DeVilbiss Health Care, Inc., 702 A.2d "
         "1228, 1232 (Del. 1997); see also SIGA Techs., Inc. v. PharmAthene, Inc., 67 A.3d "
         "330, 344 (Del. 2013); Kuhn Constr., 990 A.2d at 401. Arcadia's fraud claim rests "
         "entirely on extra-contractual sales statements\u2014precisely what \u00a7 12.1 was "
         "designed to supersede.")
    para(doc,
         "Justifiable reliance fails independently. Arcadia is a sophisticated $62-million "
         "entity that, by its own admission, conducted independent due diligence (reference "
         "calls and a proof-of-concept trial), FAC \u00b6 31, and was represented by experienced "
         "outside counsel who negotiated the MSLSA, FAC \u00b6 32. The December 9, 2021 proposal "
         "expressly told Arcadia not to \"rely solely on the information in this proposal\" "
         "and to \"conduct independent due diligence.\" FAC \u00b6 27. A plaintiff who conducts "
         "its own investigation and is represented by counsel cannot establish justifiable "
         "reliance on general promotional statements. See Presidio, 784 F.2d at 680; "
         "Hollcroft & Sedgewick, L.L.P. v. Pac. Mut. Life Ins. Co., 51 S.W.3d 573, 577 (Tex. "
         "2001). Count II must be dismissed.")

    # Count III
    heading(doc, "D.  Count III (Negligent Misrepresentation) Must Be Dismissed.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Count III fails for the same reasons as Count II, plus an independent defect. "
         "Texas recognizes negligent misrepresentation under Restatement (Second) of Torts "
         "\u00a7 552, but liability is constrained by the independent-duty requirement: the "
         "plaintiff must identify a duty of care arising from a source other than the "
         "contract. McCamish, Martin, Brown & Loeffler v. F.E. Appling Interests, 991 S.W.2d "
         "787, 791\u201392 (Tex. 1999). Meridian's relationship with Arcadia is purely "
         "contractual; the MSLSA's warranty (\u00a7 9.1) and disclaimer (\u00a7 9.4) define the "
         "scope of Meridian's obligations regarding software performance. There is no "
         "fiduciary, professional, or statutory duty independent of the MSLSA. The economic "
         "loss rule therefore bars Count III for the reasons stated in Section IV.C.2. See "
         "Chapman Custom Homes, 445 S.W.3d at 718\u201319; Brasby, 947 A.2d at 1049.")
    para(doc,
         "Count III also fails because Arcadia's damages are benefit-of-the-bargain losses, "
         "not out-of-pocket reliance losses. In Federal Land Bank Ass'n v. Sloane, 825 S.W.2d "
         "439, 442\u201343 (Tex. 1992), the Texas Supreme Court held that where the plaintiff's "
         "true complaint is that the other party \"did not deliver what was contractually "
         "promised,\" the claim sounds in contract, not tort. Arcadia seeks the $14.7 million "
         "contract value, $18.4 million in lost profits, and $6.7 million in operating "
         "costs\u2014all expectation/consequential damages, not out-of-pocket reliance losses. "
         "FAC \u00b6\u00b6 62\u201364, 96. Finally, because Count III sounds in fraud, it must satisfy "
         "Rule 9(b), Benchmark Elecs., 343 F.3d at 724, and fails for the particularity "
         "reasons stated in Section IV.C.1. The puffery and non-reliance defenses in Section "
         "IV.C.3\u20134 apply with equal force. Count III must be dismissed.")

    # Count I
    heading(doc, "E.  Count I (Breach of Contract) Must Be Dismissed or Radically Limited.",
            level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Delaware law governs Count I under \u00a7 12.7. The FAC's breach-of-contract theory "
         "is foreclosed, in whole or in substantial part, by the MSLSA's deemed-acceptance, "
         "warranty, and exclusive-remedy provisions, and its damages are barred by the "
         "consequential-damages waiver and liability cap.")

    heading(doc, "1.  Arcadia deemed-accepted the software; its post-acceptance remedies are "
                 "contractually limited.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Section 5.3 provides a 30-day acceptance-testing window, with deemed acceptance if "
         "the licensee fails to deliver written notice of material nonconformity. NexusCore "
         "went live on January 15, 2023, making February 14, 2023 the deadline. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 45, "
         "50. Arcadia delivered no written nonconformity notice within that period; its March "
         "8, 2023 email came 22 days late and was not a formal nonconformity notice. This "
         "Court has enforced an identical deemed-acceptance provision, holding that \"where "
         "the contract requires written notice, oral communications do not suffice\" and "
         "that failure to deliver timely written notice results in \"irrevocable deemed "
         "acceptance.\" Simulados, Inc. v. Canton Health Mgmt. Co., 2019 WL 4573218, at *5\u20136 "
         "(W.D. Tex. Sept. 20, 2019); see also Precision Healthcare Sols. v. Nextera Data "
         "Sys., 458 F. Supp. 3d 544, 551\u201352 (N.D. Tex. 2020) (deemed acceptance applies to "
         "latent and patent defects absent allegations of intentional concealment). The FAC "
         "alleges no intentional concealment of defects to defeat acceptance; its "
         "\"on information and belief\" concealment allegations, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 56\u201360, are "
         "conclusory. Once acceptance was deemed, Arcadia's remedies for alleged defects were "
         "channeled through the \u00a7 9.1 warranty, not through rejection or a theory of "
         "fundamental breach.")

    heading(doc, "2.  The limited warranty has expired, and its remedy is exclusive.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Section 9.1 warranted that the software would \"perform substantially in accordance "
         "with the Documentation\" for 90 days following Go-Live\u2014i.e., through April 15, "
         "2023. The sole remedy for breach of that warranty is repair/replacement or, if "
         "Meridian cannot cure within 60 days, a pro-rata refund of pre-paid license fees. "
         "\u00a7 9.1(b). Section 8.3 designates the MSLSA's remedies as exclusive. Arcadia did "
         "not deliver a formal warranty claim during the warranty period, and the four "
         "software defects identified in the support record were remediated through patches. "
         "The FAC's demand for $47.3 million\u2014including the full contract value as "
         "\"restitution,\" FAC \u00b6 62\u2014is foreclosed by these exclusive-remedy provisions. "
         "See Kana Software, Inc. v. Sealand Tech., Inc., 178 A.3d 1045, 1058\u201360 (Del. Ch. "
         "2017) (specific contractual remedy for nonconformance precludes tort- and "
         "contract-based damage theories exceeding it).")

    heading(doc, "3.  The data-migration breaches are misattributed.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "To the extent Count I rests on data-migration failures, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 43, 47, 72(d), "
         "it is foreclosed by SOW-1 \u00a7 3.2 and Exhibit C, which allocate all data-migration "
         "responsibility\u2014and liability for the accuracy, completeness, and integrity of "
         "migrated data\u2014solely to Arcadia. The FAC's own exhibit list confirms that Change "
         "Order CO-004 ($900,000) was for \"data remediation services\" addressing migration "
         "errors. FAC \u00b6 47(d). Arcadia cannot transform its own (and its integrator's) "
         "contractual responsibilities into Meridian's breach.")

    heading(doc, "4.  Alternatively, the consequential damages and punitive damages are "
                 "barred, and all damages are capped.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "If any breach-of-contract claim survives, the FAC's damages must be radically "
         "limited. Section 8.2 expressly waives \"indirect, incidental, special, "
         "consequential, punitive, or exemplary damages, including \u2026 damages for loss of "
         "profits, goodwill, use, data, or other intangible losses.\" The FAC's $18.4 million "
         "lost-profits claim, FAC \u00b6 63, its $6.7 million increased-operating-costs claim, "
         "FAC \u00b6 64, and its $5 million reputational-harm claim, FAC \u00b6 65, are "
         "consequential damages categorically barred by \u00a7 8.2. The Fifth Circuit enforces "
         "such waivers in negotiated commercial software agreements between sophisticated "
         "parties. Dresser-Rand Co. v. Virtual Automation Inc., 361 F.3d 831, 838\u201340 (5th "
         "Cir. 2004). The FAC admits Arcadia's counsel negotiated these very provisions. FAC "
         "\u00b6 32. Punitive damages are likewise barred by \u00a7 8.2. Finally, \u00a7 8.1 caps "
         "aggregate liability at the fees paid or payable in the twelve months preceding the "
         "event giving rise to liability\u2014a fraction of the $47.3 million demanded. "
         "Dresser-Rand, 361 F.3d at 838\u201339. At minimum, all consequential, punitive, and "
         "excess damages must be dismissed.")

    # ---- Conclusion ----
    heading(doc, "V.  CONCLUSION", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "For the foregoing reasons, Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. respectfully "
         "requests that the Court grant this Motion and dismiss all five counts of "
         "Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint with prejudice. In the alternative, Meridian "
         "requests that the Court dismiss all damages barred by the MSLSA's limitation-of-"
         "liability provisions, including all claims for lost profits, increased operating "
         "costs, reputational harm, punitive or treble damages, and any damages exceeding the "
         "\u00a7 8.1 aggregate-liability cap. Meridian further requests such other and further "
         "relief as the Court deems just and proper.")

    # ---- Signature ----
    para(doc, "", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Dated: January 15, 2024", space_after=6)
    para(doc, "Respectfully submitted,", space_after=10)
    para(doc, "STONEBRIDGE & CALLOWAY LLP", bold=True, space_after=6)
    para(doc, "By: /s/ Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway", space_after=0)
    sig = [
        "Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway",
        "Texas State Bar No. 24037891",
        "David Arsenault",
        "Texas State Bar No. 24098254",
        "600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2800",
        "Austin, TX 78701",
        "Telephone: (512) 555-4200",
        "Facsimile: (512) 555-4201",
        "Email: mcalloway@stonebridgecalloway.com",
        "Email: darsenault@stonebridgecalloway.com",
    ]
    for s in sig:
        para(doc, s, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Attorneys for Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.",
         italic=True, space_after=10)

    # ---- Certificate of Service ----
    heading(doc, "CERTIFICATE OF SERVICE", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "I hereby certify that on January 15, 2024, I electronically filed the foregoing "
         "Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.'s Motion to Dismiss Plaintiff's First "
         "Amended Complaint Pursuant to Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 12(b)(6) with the "
         "Clerk of Court using the CM/ECF system, which will send notification of such "
         "filing to all counsel of record, including:")
    para(doc, "Jonathan Breckenridge", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Wexford Hale LLP", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "300 West 6th Street, Suite 1500", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Austin, TX 78701", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Email: jbreckenridge@wexfordhale.com", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Counsel for Plaintiff Arcadia Health Systems, LLC", italic=True, space_after=10)
    para(doc, "/s/ Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway", space_after=0)

    # ---- Table of Authorities (does not count against page limit) ----
    doc.add_page_break()
    heading(doc, "TABLE OF AUTHORITIES", level=1, space_before=0)
    toa = [
        ("Cases", None),
        ("Abry Partners V, L.P. v. F&W Acquisition LLC, 891 A.2d 1032 (Del. Ch. 2006)", 0),

        ("Eagle Industries, Inc. v. DeVilbiss Health Care, Inc., 702 A.2d 1228 (Del. 1997)", 0),

        ("Federal Land Bank Ass'n v. Sloane, 825 S.W.2d 439 (Tex. 1992)", 0),
        ("Flaherty & Crumrine Preferred Income Fund, Inc. v. TXU Corp., 565 F.3d 200 (5th Cir. 2009)", 0),

{"command": "cat >> build_docs.py << 'PYEOF'

# ===========================================================================
# 1. MOTION TO DISMISS
# ===========================================================================
def build_motion():
    doc = base_doc()
    add_page_numbers(doc)
    caption(doc,
            "DEFENDANT MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS "
            "PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO FEDERAL RULE OF "
            "CIVIL PROCEDURE 12(b)(6)",
            response_note="Response Due: Twenty-one (21) days after filing of this Motion")

    # ---- Certificate of Conference ----
    heading(doc, "CERTIFICATE OF CONFERENCE", level=1, space_before=0)
    para(doc,
         "Pursuant to Section 3.1 of the Court's Standing Order for Civil Cases and Local "
         "Rule CV-7, counsel for Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. (\"Meridian\") "
         "conferred in good faith with counsel for Plaintiff Arcadia Health Systems, LLC "
         "(\"Arcadia\") regarding the relief sought in this Motion. The parties discussed "
         "the legal grounds raised herein, including the statutory exemption applicable to "
         "Arcadia's DTPA claim, the bar on unjust enrichment in the presence of an express "
         "contract, and the contractual and pleading deficiencies affecting Arcadia's tort "
         "and contract claims. Arcadia declined to voluntarily dismiss or narrow any claim. "
         "Because this Motion presents legal questions on which no compromise is possible, "
         "Meridian submits that further conferral would be futile and respectfully files "
         "this Motion.")

    # ---- Introduction ----
    heading(doc, "I.  INTRODUCTION", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "This is a breach-of-contract case dressed up as something more. Arcadia Health "
         "Systems, LLC\u2014a sophisticated commercial entity with approximately $62 million in "
         "annual revenue, represented by experienced outside counsel throughout an "
         "arm's-length negotiation\u2014paid $14.7 million (plus $2.35 million in mutually "
         "executed Change Orders) to license and implement Meridian's NexusCore healthcare "
         "analytics platform. Dissatisfied with the result, Arcadia now seeks $47.3 million "
         "by repackaging a single commercial dispute into five causes of action and asking "
         "the Court to disregard the comprehensive risk-allocation framework that Arcadia's "
         "own counsel negotiated and Arcadia knowingly accepted.")
    para(doc,
         "The First Amended Complaint (\"FAC\") cannot survive scrutiny under Bell Atlantic "
         "Corp. v. Twombly, 550 U.S. 544 (2007), and Ashcroft v. Iqbal, 556 U.S. 662 (2009). "
         "Several counts fail on the face of the pleadings and the governing contract as a "
         "matter of law, requiring no factual development:")
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Count V (DTPA) is barred by the statutory exemption in Texas Business & "
         "Commerce Code \u00a7 17.49(f), because the transaction exceeds $500,000 in total "
         "consideration and Arcadia is not an individual.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Count IV (Unjust Enrichment) is barred because a valid, express contract\u2014"
         "the MSLSA\u2014governs the same subject matter.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Counts II and III (Fraud and Negligent Misrepresentation) fail for multiple "
         "independent reasons: the alleged statements are non-actionable puffery; the claims "
         "are barred by the economic loss rule; the MSLSA's integration and non-reliance "
         "clause precludes reliance on extra-contractual representations; Arcadia cannot "
         "establish justifiable reliance; and the fraud claim does not satisfy Rule 9(b).",
         indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Count I (Breach of Contract) is barred or must be radically limited by the "
         "MSLSA's deemed-acceptance provision, expired limited warranty, exclusive-remedy "
         "clause, consequential-damages waiver, and aggregate-liability cap\u2014all of which "
         "Arcadia negotiated and accepted.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "Meridian therefore moves, pursuant to Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 12(b)(6), to "
         "dismiss all five counts of the FAC with prejudice, or, in the alternative, to "
         "dismiss all damages barred by the MSLSA's limitation-of-liability provisions.")

    # ---- Factual Background ----
    heading(doc, "II.  FACTUAL BACKGROUND", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "A court may consider documents referenced in the complaint and central to the "
         "plaintiff's claims without converting a Rule 12(b)(6) motion into one for summary "
         "judgment. Lormand v. US Unwired, Inc., 565 F.3d 228, 255\u201356 (5th Cir. 2009). "
         "The FAC repeatedly references and relies on the Master Software License and "
         "Services Agreement (\"MSLSA\"), Statement of Work #1 (\"SOW-1\"), the four Change "
         "Orders, Meridian's December 9, 2021 written proposal, the August 25, 2022 project "
         "status report, and Dr. Okonkwo's March 8, 2023 email. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 27, 33\u201335, "
         "44, 46, 50. Those documents, together with the FAC's own allegations, establish "
         "the following.")

    heading(doc, "A.  The MSLSA Is a Fully Negotiated, Comprehensive Risk-Allocation Agreement.",
            level=2)
    para(doc,
         "On March 15, 2022, two sophisticated commercial entities executed the MSLSA and "
         "SOW-1 for the licensing and implementation of NexusCore. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 33\u201335. "
         "Arcadia is a Texas limited liability company with approximately $62 million in "
         "annual revenue. FAC \u00b6 6. Meridian is a publicly traded Delaware corporation. "
         "FAC \u00b6 9. The FAC itself acknowledges that Arcadia \"engaged outside counsel to "
         "assist in the negotiation process\" and that Meridian \"insisted on its standard "
         "contractual terms.\" FAC \u00b6 32. The MSLSA reflects the result of that negotiation "
         "and contains a deliberate, comprehensive allocation of risk:")
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Integration and non-reliance clause (\u00a7 12.1): the MSLSA \"constitutes the "
         "entire agreement between the Parties\" and \"supersedes all prior and contemporaneous "
         "agreements, proposals, representations, and understandings, whether oral or "
         "written,\" and \"Each Party acknowledges that, in entering into this Agreement, it "
         "has not relied upon any statement, representation, warranty, or agreement of the "
         "other Party except as expressly set forth in this Agreement.\"", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Aggregate-liability cap (\u00a7 8.1): each party's aggregate liability \"shall "
         "not exceed the total fees paid or payable by Licensee during the twelve (12) month "
         "period immediately preceding the event giving rise to such liability,\" regardless "
         "of the form of action.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Consequential-damages waiver (\u00a7 8.2): neither party is liable for "
         "\"indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive, or exemplary damages, "
         "including \u2026 damages for loss of profits, goodwill, use, data, or other intangible "
         "losses.\"", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Exclusive remedies (\u00a7 8.3): the MSLSA's remedies are the licensee's \"sole "
         "and exclusive remedies for any breach.\"", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Limited warranty and disclaimer (\u00a7\u00a7 9.1, 9.4): a 90-day warranty that "
         "the software will \"perform substantially in accordance with the Documentation,\" "
         "with repair/replacement or a pro-rata refund as the sole remedy; all other "
         "warranties, express or implied, are disclaimed.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Deemed acceptance (\u00a7 5.3): a 30-day acceptance-testing window following "
         "deployment, with acceptance \"deemed\" if the licensee fails to deliver written "
         "notice of material nonconformity.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "\u2022  Governing law (\u00a7 12.7): Delaware law, without regard to conflict-of-law "
         "principles.", indent=0.25)

    heading(doc, "B.  Data Migration Was Solely Arcadia's Responsibility.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The FAC's central narrative\u2014that Meridian \"failed to properly plan and execute "
         "the data migration,\" FAC \u00b6 43\u2014is foreclosed by the contract. Section 3.2 of "
         "SOW-1 provides that Arcadia \"shall be solely responsible for all data migration "
         "activities, including extraction, transformation, and loading of legacy data into "
         "NexusCore,\" and that Meridian's role was limited to \"advisory consulting services\" "
         "with \"no responsibility for the accuracy, completeness, or integrity of migrated "
         "data.\" Exhibit C to the MSLSA reiterates that Meridian \"shall have no "
         "responsibility or liability\" for data migrated by Arcadia or its contractors. The "
         "FAC nonetheless attributes to Meridian the failures of Arcadia's own independently "
         "retained systems integrator, Linden Park Consulting, LLC. See FAC \u00b6\u00b6 43, 47, "
         "72(d).")

    heading(doc, "C.  Arcadia Conducted Independent Due Diligence and Accepted the Risk "
                 "Allocation Knowingly.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The FAC admits that Arcadia's CTO, Martin Schreiber, \"conducted certain independent "
         "due diligence, including reference calls with two existing Meridian healthcare "
         "clients\" before contract execution. FAC \u00b6 31. The FAC further admits that "
         "Arcadia \"engaged outside counsel to assist in the negotiation process\" and "
         "attempted (unsuccessfully) to negotiate \"enhanced limitation of liability "
         "provisions and expanded warranty protections.\" FAC \u00b6 32. And the FAC concedes "
         "that Meridian's December 9, 2021 written proposal \"contained certain general "
         "disclaimers.\" FAC \u00b6 27. Those disclaimers expressly stated that \"[e]stimated "
         "timelines and performance metrics are provided for planning purposes only and do "
         "not constitute guarantees,\" that \"Meridian does not warrant that integration with "
         "any particular EHR system will be seamless, error-free, or achievable within any "
         "specific timeframe,\" and that Arcadia \"should not rely solely on the information "
         "in this proposal.\" These admissions defeat any claim of justifiable reliance on "
         "pre-contractual sales statements.")

    heading(doc, "D.  Arcadia Deemed-Accepted the Software and Its Warranty Has Expired.",
            level=2)
    para(doc,
         "NexusCore achieved Go-Live on January 15, 2023. FAC \u00b6 45. Under \u00a7 5.3, Arcadia "
         "had 30 days\u2014until February 14, 2023\u2014to deliver written notice of any material "
         "nonconformity. Arcadia did not do so. Its first written communication about "
         "performance\u2014Dr. Okonkwo's March 8, 2023 email\u2014was sent 22 days after the "
         "acceptance window closed and reported only \"intermittent latency issues\" and "
         "\"occasional report generation errors.\" FAC \u00b6 50. Acceptance was therefore "
         "deemed granted as of February 14, 2023. The \u00a7 9.1 limited warranty ran for 90 "
         "days from Go-Live and expired on April 15, 2023, with repair/replacement or a "
         "pro-rata refund as the sole remedy.")

    # ---- Legal Standard ----
    heading(doc, "III.  LEGAL STANDARD", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "To survive a Rule 12(b)(6) motion, a complaint must contain sufficient factual "
         "matter to \"state a claim to relief that is plausible on its face.\" Twombly, 550 "
         "U.S. at 570; Iqbal, 556 U.S. at 678. The Court \"accept[s] all well-pleaded facts "
         "as true,\" but need not \"accept as true \u2026 legal conclusions couched as factual "
         "allegations.\" Lormand, 565 F.3d at 255\u201356. \"Threadbare recitals of the elements "
         "of a cause of action, supported by mere conclusory statements, do not suffice.\" "
         "Iqbal, 556 U.S. at 678. As this Court's Standing Order confirms, claims sounding in "
         "fraud must satisfy Rule 9(b)'s particularity requirement, including the \"who, "
         "what, when, where, and how\" of the alleged fraud. Standing Order \u00a7 4.2; "
         "Benchmark Elecs., Inc. v. J.M. Huber Corp., 343 F.3d 719, 724 (5th Cir. 2003).")
    para(doc,
         "Meridian preserved its Rule 12(b)(6) defenses in its Answer, including the First "
         "Affirmative Defense (failure to state a claim) and an express reservation of the "
         "right to move under Rule 12(b)(6). This Motion is therefore timely under the "
         "Court's Standing Order \u00a7 3.4 and the Scheduling Order's January 15, 2024 "
         "dispositive-motion deadline. The Court may consider the MSLSA, SOW-1, and Change "
         "Orders, which are referenced in and central to the FAC. Lormand, 565 F.3d at "
         "255\u201356.")

    # ---- Argument ----
    heading(doc, "IV.  ARGUMENT", level=1)

    # Count V
    heading(doc, "A.  Count V (DTPA) Must Be Dismissed Under the \u00a7 17.49(f) Statutory "
                 "Exemption.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Count V fails as a matter of law on the face of the pleadings. Section 17.49(f) of "
         "the Texas Business & Commerce Code exempts from DTPA coverage any \"cause of action "
         "arising from a transaction, a project, or a set of transactions \u2026 relating to the "
         "same project, involving total consideration by the consumer of more than $500,000,\" "
         "unless the consumer is an individual. Tex. Bus. & Com. Code \u00a7 17.49(f). Both "
         "conditions are satisfied here, and both are established by the FAC's own "
         "allegations.")
    para(doc,
         "First, Arcadia is not an individual. The FAC alleges that Arcadia is a Texas "
         "limited liability company. FAC \u00b6 6. Second, the total consideration vastly "
         "exceeds $500,000. The FAC alleges a base contract value of $14,700,000, FAC "
         "\u00b6\u00b6 34, 69, plus four Change Orders totaling $2,350,000, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 46\u201347, "
         "100\u2014a total of $17,050,000, more than thirty-four times the statutory threshold. "
         "The exemption is determined by the total consideration involved in the transaction, "
         "not by the amount of damages claimed. See PPG Indus., Inc. v. JMB/Houston Ctrs. "
         "Partners Ltd., 146 S.W.3d 79, 89 (Tex. App.\u2014Houston [1st Dist.] 2004, no pet.). "
         "Because the \u00a7 17.49(f) exemption is dispositive and turns solely on facts alleged "
         "in the FAC and a statute of which the Court may take judicial notice, Count V must "
         "be dismissed.")
    para(doc,
         "Count V fails for additional, independent reasons. Arcadia is not a \"consumer\" "
         "within the DTPA's meaning: this is a sophisticated, $14.7 million enterprise-"
         "software transaction negotiated at arm's length between two commercial entities, "
         "each represented by counsel\u2014not the consumer transaction the DTPA was enacted to "
         "protect. See Riverside Nat'l Bank v. Lewis, 603 S.W.2d 169, 174\u201375 (Tex. 1980). "
         "Moreover, the alleged misrepresentations are non-actionable puffery (see Section "
         "IV.C.3, below), and Arcadia cannot establish \"producing cause\" because the "
         "implementation delays and data-quality issues it complains of were caused by its "
         "own failures and those of its systems integrator, not by any sales statement. See "
         "Melody Home Mfg. Co. v. Barnes, 741 S.W.2d 349, 351\u201352 (Tex. 1987).")

    # Count IV
    heading(doc, "B.  Count IV (Unjust Enrichment) Must Be Dismissed Because an Express "
                 "Contract Governs the Same Subject Matter.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Under both Texas and Delaware law, a claim for unjust enrichment (or quantum "
         "meruit) is unavailable where a valid, express contract governs the subject matter "
         "of the dispute. Fortune Prod. Co. v. Conoco, Inc., 52 S.W.3d 671, 684 (Tex. 2000); "
         "Nemec v. Shrader, 991 A.2d 1120, 1130 (Del. 2010). The \"same subject matter\" test "
         "is applied broadly: if the contract addresses the general subject area of the "
         "dispute, unjust enrichment is barred. Excess Underwriters at Lloyd's v. Frank's "
         "Casing Crew & Rental Tools, Inc., 246 S.W.3d 42, 59\u201360 (Tex. 2008). Critically, "
         "the fact that contractual limitations (such as a liability cap or consequential-"
         "damages waiver) may limit recovery under the contract does not create a \"gap\" "
         "that unjust enrichment can fill. Id. at 59. \"Equity follows the law\"; where "
         "parties have allocated risk through contract, equity will not rewrite the bargain.")
    para(doc,
         "Count IV is irreconcilably inconsistent with Count I. In Count I, Arcadia alleges "
         "that a valid, enforceable MSLSA exists and that Meridian breached it. FAC \u00b6 69. "
         "Arcadia does not contend that the MSLSA is void or unenforceable\u2014to the contrary, "
         "it relies on the MSLSA as the foundation of its breach-of-contract claim. Count IV "
         "is pleaded only \"in the alternative \u2026 to the extent the MSLSA or any provision "
         "thereof is found to be unenforceable.\" FAC \u00b6 99. But Arcadia never alleges any "
         "ground of unenforceability. The MSLSA indisputably covers the entire scope of the "
         "parties' software-licensing and implementation relationship\u2014the very payments "
         "Arcadia seeks to recover as restitution. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 100\u2013103. Under Fortune "
         "Production and Excess Underwriters, unjust enrichment is unavailable as a matter of "
         "law, and Count IV must be dismissed.")

    # Count II
    heading(doc, "C.  Count II (Fraud) Must Be Dismissed on Four Independent Grounds.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MSLSA's \u00a7 12.7 designates Delaware law to govern the contract. To the extent "
         "Texas law governs the tort claims under the most-significant-relationship test, the "
         "fraud claim fails under either body of law, for the reasons below.")

    heading(doc, "1.  The FAC does not satisfy Rule 9(b).", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Fraud must be pleaded with particularity, specifying \"the statements contended to "
         "be fraudulent, identify[ing] the speaker, stat[ing] when and where the statements "
         "were made, and explain[ing] why the statements were fraudulent.\" Benchmark "
         "Elecs., 343 F.3d at 724. While the FAC identifies James Poletti and approximate "
         "dates, it does not plead why the statements were false when made. Its theory is "
         "entirely post-hoc: because NexusCore allegedly performed poorly after Go-Live, the "
         "pre-sale statements must have been false. That reasoning is insufficient. See "
         "Flaherty & Crumrine Preferred Income Fund, Inc. v. TXU Corp., 565 F.3d 200, 207\u201308 "
         "(5th Cir. 2009) (post-hoc product failure does not support an inference of "
         "scienter absent internal documents, contemporaneous admissions, or motive). The "
         "FAC's scienter allegations\u2014that Meridian \"knew or should have known\" the "
         "statements were false, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 30, 81\u2014are precisely the conclusory averments "
         "Rule 9(b) forbids. The concealment allegations (\u00b6\u00b6 56\u201360) are pleaded "
         "\"on information and belief\" without any factual basis, which this Court's "
         "Standing Order \u00a7 4.2 holds insufficient.")

    heading(doc, "2.  The economic loss rule bars the fraud claim.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Under Delaware law, \"where a plaintiff's claims arise solely from the contractual "
         "relationship between the parties, and the damages sought are exclusively economic "
         "losses \u2026 the economic loss doctrine bars recovery in tort.\" Brasby v. Morris "
         "Dynamics, Inc., 947 A.2d 1042, 1049 (Del. 2008). The doctrine applies with "
         "particular force where sophisticated parties represented by counsel negotiated a "
         "comprehensive written agreement containing an integration clause and warranty "
         "disclaimers. Kuhn Constr., Inc. v. Diamond State Port Corp., 990 A.2d 393, 401\u201302 "
         "(Del. 2010). Under Texas law, the rule bars tort recovery where (1) the losses are "
         "purely economic, (2) they relate to the subject matter of a contract, and (3) the "
         "defendant owes no duty independent of the contract. Chapman Custom Homes, Inc. v. "
         "Dallas Plumbing Co., 445 S.W.3d 716, 718\u201319 (Tex. 2014); Sharyland Water Supply "
         "Corp. v. City of Alton, 354 S.W.3d 407, 415\u201316 (Tex. 2011).")
    para(doc,
         "All three conditions are met. Arcadia's damages\u2014$14.7 million in contract value, "
         "$18.4 million in lost profits, $6.7 million in operating costs, and $5 million in "
         "reputational harm, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 61\u201366\u2014are purely economic. They all relate to "
         "NexusCore, the subject of the MSLSA. And Meridian owes no duty to Arcadia "
         "independent of the MSLSA: there is no fiduciary relationship, no professional duty, "
         "and no statutory duty beyond the contract. The alleged misrepresentations concern "
         "the very quality and performance of NexusCore that the MSLSA addresses through its "
         "warranty (\u00a7 9.1), disclaimer (\u00a7 9.4), and integration clause (\u00a7 12.1). "
         "Arcadia's fraud claim is, at bottom, a claim that Meridian did not deliver software "
         "that performed as promised\u2014\"the very essence of a breach of contract claim.\" "
         "Sharyland, 354 S.W.3d at 415. That repackaging is barred.")

    heading(doc, "3.  The alleged statements are non-actionable puffery.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "The Fifth Circuit holds that \"a general claim of superiority that is so vague that "
         "it can be understood only as the seller's opinion\" is non-actionable puffery. "
         "Pizza Hut, Inc. v. Papa John's Int'l, Inc., 227 F.3d 489, 497 (5th Cir. 2000); see "
         "also Castrol Inc. v. Pennzoil Co., 987 F.2d 939, 945 (3d Cir. 1993). "
         "Forward-looking statements about a product's expected performance, made in a sales "
         "context, are likewise puffery upon which a sophisticated buyer cannot reasonably "
         "rely. Presidio Enters., Inc. v. Warner Bros. Distributing Corp., 784 F.2d 674, "
         "679\u201380 (5th Cir. 1986). The FAC's lead examples are textbook puffery: \"industry-"
         "leading performance for healthcare analytics\" and \"30-40% improvement in "
         "reporting efficiency\" are vague, subjective claims of superiority and generalized "
         "projections. FAC \u00b6 23. The \"seamless integration\" statement, FAC \u00b6 25, is "
         "directly contradicted by Meridian's December 9, 2021 proposal, which expressly "
         "disclaimed that integration would be \"seamless, error-free, or achievable within "
         "any specific timeframe.\" FAC \u00b6 27. Puffery remains non-actionable even when "
         "embedded in a sales presentation. Pizza Hut, 227 F.3d at 498\u201399.")

    heading(doc, "4.  The integration and non-reliance clause bars reliance, and reliance "
                 "was not justifiable.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Section 12.1 is both a comprehensive integration clause and an express non-reliance "
         "disclaimer: the parties acknowledged that they \"ha[ve] not relied upon any "
         "statement, representation, warranty, or agreement of the other Party except as "
         "expressly set forth in this Agreement.\" Under Delaware law, \"a party to a "
         "contract cannot promise, in a clear integration clause of a negotiated agreement, "
         "that it is not relying on promises or representations made by the other party "
         "outside of the agreement, and then assert a claim for fraud based on those very "
         "representations.\" Eagle Indus., Inc. v. DeVilbiss Health Care, Inc., 702 A.2d "
         "1228, 1232 (Del. 1997); see also SIGA Techs., Inc. v. PharmAthene, Inc., 67 A.3d "
         "330, 344 (Del. 2013); Kuhn Constr., 990 A.2d at 401. Arcadia's fraud claim rests "
         "entirely on extra-contractual sales statements\u2014precisely what \u00a7 12.1 was "
         "designed to supersede.")
    para(doc,
         "Justifiable reliance fails independently. Arcadia is a sophisticated $62-million "
         "entity that, by its own admission, conducted independent due diligence (reference "
         "calls and a proof-of-concept trial), FAC \u00b6 31, and was represented by experienced "
         "outside counsel who negotiated the MSLSA, FAC \u00b6 32. The December 9, 2021 proposal "
         "expressly told Arcadia not to \"rely solely on the information in this proposal\" "
         "and to \"conduct independent due diligence.\" FAC \u00b6 27. A plaintiff who conducts "
         "its own investigation and is represented by counsel cannot establish justifiable "
         "reliance on general promotional statements. See Presidio, 784 F.2d at 680; "
         "Hollcroft & Sedgewick, L.L.P. v. Pac. Mut. Life Ins. Co., 51 S.W.3d 573, 577 (Tex. "
         "2001). Count II must be dismissed.")

    # Count III
    heading(doc, "D.  Count III (Negligent Misrepresentation) Must Be Dismissed.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Count III fails for the same reasons as Count II, plus an independent defect. "
         "Texas recognizes negligent misrepresentation under Restatement (Second) of Torts "
         "\u00a7 552, but liability is constrained by the independent-duty requirement: the "
         "plaintiff must identify a duty of care arising from a source other than the "
         "contract. McCamish, Martin, Brown & Loeffler v. F.E. Appling Interests, 991 S.W.2d "
         "787, 791\u201392 (Tex. 1999). Meridian's relationship with Arcadia is purely "
         "contractual; the MSLSA's warranty (\u00a7 9.1) and disclaimer (\u00a7 9.4) define the "
         "scope of Meridian's obligations regarding software performance. There is no "
         "fiduciary, professional, or statutory duty independent of the MSLSA. The economic "
         "loss rule therefore bars Count III for the reasons stated in Section IV.C.2. See "
         "Chapman Custom Homes, 445 S.W.3d at 718\u201319; Brasby, 947 A.2d at 1049.")
    para(doc,
         "Count III also fails because Arcadia's damages are benefit-of-the-bargain losses, "
         "not out-of-pocket reliance losses. In Federal Land Bank Ass'n v. Sloane, 825 S.W.2d "
         "439, 442\u201343 (Tex. 1992), the Texas Supreme Court held that where the plaintiff's "
         "true complaint is that the other party \"did not deliver what was contractually "
         "promised,\" the claim sounds in contract, not tort. Arcadia seeks the $14.7 million "
         "contract value, $18.4 million in lost profits, and $6.7 million in operating "
         "costs\u2014all expectation/consequential damages, not out-of-pocket reliance losses. "
         "FAC \u00b6\u00b6 62\u201364, 96. Finally, because Count III sounds in fraud, it must satisfy "
         "Rule 9(b), Benchmark Elecs., 343 F.3d at 724, and fails for the particularity "
         "reasons stated in Section IV.C.1. The puffery and non-reliance defenses in Section "
         "IV.C.3\u20134 apply with equal force. Count III must be dismissed.")

    # Count I
    heading(doc, "E.  Count I (Breach of Contract) Must Be Dismissed or Radically Limited.",
            level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Delaware law governs Count I under \u00a7 12.7. The FAC's breach-of-contract theory "
         "is foreclosed, in whole or in substantial part, by the MSLSA's deemed-acceptance, "
         "warranty, and exclusive-remedy provisions, and its damages are barred by the "
         "consequential-damages waiver and liability cap.")

    heading(doc, "1.  Arcadia deemed-accepted the software; its post-acceptance remedies are "
                 "contractually limited.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Section 5.3 provides a 30-day acceptance-testing window, with deemed acceptance if "
         "the licensee fails to deliver written notice of material nonconformity. NexusCore "
         "went live on January 15, 2023, making February 14, 2023 the deadline. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 45, "
         "50. Arcadia delivered no written nonconformity notice within that period; its March "
         "8, 2023 email came 22 days late and was not a formal nonconformity notice. This "
         "Court has enforced an identical deemed-acceptance provision, holding that \"where "
         "the contract requires written notice, oral communications do not suffice\" and "
         "that failure to deliver timely written notice results in \"irrevocable deemed "
         "acceptance.\" Simulados, Inc. v. Canton Health Mgmt. Co., 2019 WL 4573218, at *5\u20136 "
         "(W.D. Tex. Sept. 20, 2019); see also Precision Healthcare Sols. v. Nextera Data "
         "Sys., 458 F. Supp. 3d 544, 551\u201352 (N.D. Tex. 2020) (deemed acceptance applies to "
         "latent and patent defects absent allegations of intentional concealment). The FAC "
         "alleges no intentional concealment of defects to defeat acceptance; its "
         "\"on information and belief\" concealment allegations, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 56\u201360, are "
         "conclusory. Once acceptance was deemed, Arcadia's remedies for alleged defects were "
         "channeled through the \u00a7 9.1 warranty, not through rejection or a theory of "
         "fundamental breach.")

    heading(doc, "2.  The limited warranty has expired, and its remedy is exclusive.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "Section 9.1 warranted that the software would \"perform substantially in accordance "
         "with the Documentation\" for 90 days following Go-Live\u2014i.e., through April 15, "
         "2023. The sole remedy for breach of that warranty is repair/replacement or, if "
         "Meridian cannot cure within 60 days, a pro-rata refund of pre-paid license fees. "
         "\u00a7 9.1(b). Section 8.3 designates the MSLSA's remedies as exclusive. Arcadia did "
         "not deliver a formal warranty claim during the warranty period, and the four "
         "software defects identified in the support record were remediated through patches. "
         "The FAC's demand for $47.3 million\u2014including the full contract value as "
         "\"restitution,\" FAC \u00b6 62\u2014is foreclosed by these exclusive-remedy provisions. "
         "See Kana Software, Inc. v. Sealand Tech., Inc., 178 A.3d 1045, 1058\u201360 (Del. Ch. "
         "2017) (specific contractual remedy for nonconformance precludes tort- and "
         "contract-based damage theories exceeding it).")

    heading(doc, "3.  The data-migration breaches are misattributed.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "To the extent Count I rests on data-migration failures, FAC \u00b6\u00b6 43, 47, 72(d), "
         "it is foreclosed by SOW-1 \u00a7 3.2 and Exhibit C, which allocate all data-migration "
         "responsibility\u2014and liability for the accuracy, completeness, and integrity of "
         "migrated data\u2014solely to Arcadia. The FAC's own exhibit list confirms that Change "
         "Order CO-004 ($900,000) was for \"data remediation services\" addressing migration "
         "errors. FAC \u00b6 47(d). Arcadia cannot transform its own (and its integrator's) "
         "contractual responsibilities into Meridian's breach.")

    heading(doc, "4.  Alternatively, the consequential damages and punitive damages are "
                 "barred, and all damages are capped.", level=3)
    para(doc,
         "If any breach-of-contract claim survives, the FAC's damages must be radically "
         "limited. Section 8.2 expressly waives \"indirect, incidental, special, "
         "consequential, punitive, or exemplary damages, including \u2026 damages for loss of "
         "profits, goodwill, use, data, or other intangible losses.\" The FAC's $18.4 million "
         "lost-profits claim, FAC \u00b6 63, its $6.7 million increased-operating-costs claim, "
         "FAC \u00b6 64, and its $5 million reputational-harm claim, FAC \u00b6 65, are "
         "consequential damages categorically barred by \u00a7 8.2. The Fifth Circuit enforces "
         "such waivers in negotiated commercial software agreements between sophisticated "
         "parties. Dresser-Rand Co. v. Virtual Automation Inc., 361 F.3d 831, 838\u201340 (5th "
         "Cir. 2004). The FAC admits Arcadia's counsel negotiated these very provisions. FAC "
         "\u00b6 32. Punitive damages are likewise barred by \u00a7 8.2. Finally, \u00a7 8.1 caps "
         "aggregate liability at the fees paid or payable in the twelve months preceding the "
         "event giving rise to liability\u2014a fraction of the $47.3 million demanded. "
         "Dresser-Rand, 361 F.3d at 838\u201339. At minimum, all consequential, punitive, and "
         "excess damages must be dismissed.")

    # ---- Conclusion ----
    heading(doc, "V.  CONCLUSION", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "For the foregoing reasons, Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. respectfully "
         "requests that the Court grant this Motion and dismiss all five counts of "
         "Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint with prejudice. In the alternative, Meridian "
         "requests that the Court dismiss all damages barred by the MSLSA's limitation-of-"
         "liability provisions, including all claims for lost profits, increased operating "
         "costs, reputational harm, punitive or treble damages, and any damages exceeding the "
         "\u00a7 8.1 aggregate-liability cap. Meridian further requests such other and further "
         "relief as the Court deems just and proper.")

    # ---- Signature ----
    para(doc, "", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Dated: January 15, 2024", space_after=6)
    para(doc, "Respectfully submitted,", space_after=10)
    para(doc, "STONEBRIDGE & CALLOWAY LLP", bold=True, space_after=6)
    para(doc, "By: /s/ Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway", space_after=0)
    sig = [
        "Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway",
        "Texas State Bar No. 24037891",
        "David Arsenault",
        "Texas State Bar No. 24098254",
        "600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2800",
        "Austin, TX 78701",
        "Telephone: (512) 555-4200",
        "Facsimile: (512) 555-4201",
        "Email: mcalloway@stonebridgecalloway.com",
        "Email: darsenault@stonebridgecalloway.com",
    ]
    for s in sig:
        para(doc, s, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Attorneys for Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.",
         italic=True, space_after=10)

    # ---- Certificate of Service ----
    heading(doc, "CERTIFICATE OF SERVICE", level=1)
    para(doc,
         "I hereby certify that on January 15, 2024, I electronically filed the foregoing "
         "Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.'s Motion to Dismiss Plaintiff's First "
         "Amended Complaint Pursuant to Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 12(b)(6) with the "
         "Clerk of Court using the CM/ECF system, which will send notification of such "
         "filing to all counsel of record, including:")
    para(doc, "Jonathan Breckenridge", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Wexford Hale LLP", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "300 West 6th Street, Suite 1500", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Austin, TX 78701", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Email: jbreckenridge@wexfordhale.com", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Counsel for Plaintiff Arcadia Health Systems, LLC", italic=True, space_after=10)
    para(doc, "/s/ Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway", space_after=0)

    # ---- Table of Authorities (does not count against page limit) ----
    doc.add_page_break()
    heading(doc, "TABLE OF AUTHORITIES", level=1, space_before=0)
    toa = [
        ("Cases", None),
        ("Abry Partners V, L.P. v. F&W Acquisition LLC, 891 A.2d 1032 (Del. Ch. 2006)", 0),
        ("Ashcroft v. Iqbal, 556 U.S. 662 (2009)", 0),
        ("Bell Atlantic Corp. v. Twombly, 550 U.S. 544 (2007)", 0),
        ("Benchmark Electronics, Inc. v. J.M. Huber Corp., 343 F.3d 719 (5th Cir. 2003)", 0),
        ("Brasby v. Morris Dynamics, Inc., 947 A.2d 1042 (Del. 2008)", 0),
        ("Castrol Inc. v. Pennzoil Co., 987 F.2d 939 (3d Cir. 1993)", 0),
        ("Chapman Custom Homes, Inc. v. Dallas Plumbing Co., 445 S.W.3d 716 (Tex. 2014)", 0),
        ("Dorsey v. Portfolio Equities, Inc., 540 F.3d 333 (5th Cir. 2008)", 0),
        ("Dresser-Rand Co. v. Virtual Automation Inc., 361 F.3d 831 (5th Cir. 2004)", 0),
        ("Eagle Industries, Inc. v. DeVilbiss Health Care, Inc., 702 A.2d 1228 (Del. 1997)", 0),
        ("Excess Underwriters at Lloyd's v. Frank's Casing Crew & Rental Tools, Inc., 246 S.W.3d 42 (Tex. 2008)", 0),
        ("Federal Land Bank Ass'n v. Sloane, 825 S.W.2d 439 (Tex. 1992)", 0),
        ("Flaherty & Crumrine Preferred Income Fund, Inc. v. TXU Corp., 565 F.3d 200 (5th Cir. 2009)", 0),
        ("Fortune Production Co. v. Conoco, Inc., 52 S.W.3d 671 (Tex. 2000)", 0),
        ("Hollcroft & Sedgewick, L.L.P. v. Pacific Mutual Life Insurance Co., 51 S.W.3d 573 (Tex. 2001)", 0),
        ("Kana Software, Inc. v. Sealand Technology, Inc., 178 A.3d 1045 (Del. Ch. 2017)", 0),
        ("Kuhn Construction, Inc. v. Diamond State Port Corp., 990 A.2d 393 (Del. 2010)", 0),
        ("Lormand v. US Unwired, Inc., 565 F.3d 228 (5th Cir. 2009)", 0),
        ("McCamish, Martin, Brown & Loeffler v. F.E. Appling Interests, 991 S.W.2d 787 (Tex. 1999)", 0),
        ("Melody Home Manufacturing Co. v. Barnes, 741 S.W.2d 349 (Tex. 1987)", 0),
        ("Nemec v. Shrader, 991 A.2d 1120 (Del. 2010)", 0),
        ("Pizza Hut, Inc. v. Papa John's International, Inc., 227 F.3d 489 (5th Cir. 2000)", 0),
        ("PPG Industries, Inc. v. JMB/Houston Centers Partners Ltd., 146 S.W.3d 79 (Tex. App.\u2014Houston [1st Dist.] 2004, no pet.)", 0),
        ("Precision Healthcare Solutions v. Nextera Data Systems, 458 F. Supp. 3d 544 (N.D. Tex. 2020)", 0),
        ("Presidio Enterprises, Inc. v. Warner Bros. Distributing Corp., 784 F.2d 674 (5th Cir. 1986)", 0),
        ("Riverside National Bank v. Lewis, 603 S.W.2d 169 (Tex. 1980)", 0),
        ("Sharyland Water Supply Corp. v. City of Alton, 354 S.W.3d 407 (Tex. 2011)", 0),
        ("SIGA Technologies, Inc. v. PharmAthene, Inc., 67 A.3d 330 (Del. 2013)", 0),
        ("Simulados, Inc. v. Canton Health Management Co., 2019 WL 4573218 (W.D. Tex. Sept. 20, 2019)", 0),
        ("Statutes and Rules", None),
        ("Fed. R. Civ. P. 9(b)", 0),
        ("Fed. R. Civ. P. 12(b)(6)", 0),
        ("Tex. Bus. & Com. Code \u00a7\u00a7 17.41\u201317.63", 0),
        ("Tex. Bus. & Com. Code \u00a7 17.49(f)", 0),
        ("Other Authorities", None),
        ("Standing Order for Civil Cases Before the Hon. Catherine M. Alvarez, \u00a7\u00a7 3.1, 3.4, 4.2 (W.D. Tex. Sept. 1, 2023)", 0),
    ]
    for entry, _ in toa:
        if _ is None:
            para(doc, entry, bold=True, italic=True, space_before=4, space_after=0)
        else:
            p = doc.add_paragraph()
            p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches(0.5)
            p.paragraph_format.first_line_indent = Inches(-0.25)
            p.paragraph_format.line_spacing_rule = WD_LINE_SPACING.SINGLE
            p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0)
            r = p.add_run(entry); _set_run(r, size=11)

    save(doc, "motion-to-dismiss.docx")
    return doc

print("motion function defined")
PYEOF
echo "appended motion""}
