tool_calls.arguments:
{"command": "cat >> build_docs.py << 'PYEOF'

# ===========================================================================
# 2. PROPOSED ORDER
# ===========================================================================
def build_order():
    doc = base_doc()
    add_page_numbers(doc)
    para(doc, "UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT", bold=True, align="center", caps=True, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "WESTERN DISTRICT OF TEXAS", bold=True, align="center", caps=True, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "AUSTIN DIVISION", bold=True, align="center", caps=True, space_after=6)
    t = doc.add_table(rows=4, cols=2)
    cells = [
        ("ARCADIA HEALTH SYSTEMS, LLC,", "Civil Action No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA"),
        ("Plaintiff,", ""),
        ("v.", ""),
        ("MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS, INC.,", ""),
    ]
    for i,(l,r) in enumerate(cells):
        lc = t.cell(i,0); rc = t.cell(i,1)
        lp = lc.paragraphs[0]; lp.paragraph_format.line_spacing_rule = WD_LINE_SPACING.SINGLE
        lp.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0)
        if l:
            rr = lp.add_run(l); _set_run(rr, size=12)
        rp = rc.paragraphs[0]; rp.paragraph_format.line_spacing_rule = WD_LINE_SPACING.SINGLE
        rp.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(0)
        if r:
            rr = rp.add_run(r); _set_run(rr, size=12)
    a = t.cell(0,1); b = t.cell(3,1); a.merge(b)
    para(doc, "Defendant.", space_after=8)
    para(doc, "\u00a7 1", bold=True, align="center", space_after=6)

    heading(doc, "ORDER", level=1, space_before=0)
    para(doc,
         "On this day came on to be considered Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.'s "
         "Motion to Dismiss Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint Pursuant to Federal Rule of "
         "Civil Procedure 12(b)(6) (Dkt. No. [___]), and the Court, having reviewed the "
         "Motion, the First Amended Complaint, the governing Master Software License and "
         "Services Agreement and related documents, and the applicable law, is of the "
         "opinion that the Motion should be granted.")

    heading(doc, "IT IS THEREFORE ORDERED, ADJUDGED, AND DECREED that Defendant Meridian "
                 "Cloud Solutions, Inc.'s Motion to Dismiss (Dkt. No. [___]) is GRANTED.",
            level=2, space_before=4)

    para(doc,
         "IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that all five counts of Plaintiff Arcadia Health Systems, "
         "LLC's First Amended Complaint are DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE, namely:")
    para(doc, "(1)  Count I \u2014 Breach of Contract;", indent=0.5, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "(2)  Count II \u2014 Fraud;", indent=0.5, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "(3)  Count III \u2014 Negligent Misrepresentation;", indent=0.5, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "(4)  Count IV \u2014 Unjust Enrichment; and", indent=0.5, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "(5)  Count V \u2014 Violation of the Texas Deceptive Trade Practices\u2013Consumer "
              "Protection Act, Tex. Bus. & Com. Code \u00a7\u00a7 17.41\u201317.63.", indent=0.5)

    para(doc,
         "IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that, in the event the Court does not dismiss Count I in its "
         "entirety, all of Plaintiff's claims for consequential, incidental, special, "
         "punitive, and exemplary damages\u2014including claims for lost profits, increased "
         "operating costs, reputational harm, and treble damages\u2014are DISMISSED WITH "
         "PREJUDICE as barred by Section 8.2 of the Master Software License and Services "
         "Agreement, and all remaining damages are limited to the aggregate-liability cap "
         "set forth in Section 8.1 of that Agreement.")

    para(doc,
         "IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Plaintiff Arcadia Health Systems, LLC shall take nothing "
         "by its First Amended Complaint, that all claims against Defendant Meridian Cloud "
         "Solutions, Inc. are DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE, and that Defendant Meridian Cloud "
         "Solutions, Inc. be go hence without day and recover its costs of court, for which "
         "execution may issue, together with such other and further relief to which "
         "Defendant may be entitled under the Master Software License and Services Agreement "
         "or applicable law.")

    para(doc,
         "IT IS FINALLY ORDERED that this Order is final and appealable as to all claims and "
         "all parties, and that the Clerk of Court is directed to close this case.")

    para(doc, "", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "SIGNED on this _____ day of ____________, 2024.", space_after=20)
    para(doc, "_______________________________________", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "THE HONORABLE CATHERINE M. ALVAREZ", bold=True, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "United States District Judge", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Western District of Texas", space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Austin Division", space_after=0)

    save(doc, "proposed-order.docx")
    return doc

# ===========================================================================
# 3. COVER MEMO (internal, threshold issues)
# ===========================================================================
def build_memo():
    doc = base_doc()
    add_page_numbers(doc)
    # Firm letterhead
    para(doc, "STONEBRIDGE & CALLOWAY LLP", bold=True, align="center", caps=True, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2800  \u2022  Austin, Texas 78701",
         align="center", size=11, space_after=0)
    para(doc, "Telephone (512) 555-4200  \u2022  Facsimile (512) 555-4201",
         align="center", size=11, space_after=8)
    para(doc, "PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL  \u2022  ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT",
         bold=True, align="center", size=11, space_after=10)

    heading(doc, "MEMORANDUM", level=1, space_before=0)
    # memo header table
    hdr = [
        ("TO:", "Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway, Lead Partner; David Arsenault, Senior Associate"),
        ("FROM:", "Litigation Team, Stonebridge & Calloway LLP"),
        ("DATE:", "January 12, 2024"),
        ("RE:", "Threshold Issues \u2014 Filing of Rule 12(b)(6) Motion to Dismiss; "
                "Arcadia Health Systems, LLC v. Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc., "
                "No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA (W.D. Tex., Austin Div.)"),
    ]
    for label, val in hdr:
        p = doc.add_paragraph()
        p.paragraph_format.line_spacing_rule = WD_LINE_SPACING.SINGLE
        p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        r1 = p.add_run(label + "  "); _set_run(r1, bold=True)
        r2 = p.add_run(val); _set_run(r2)
    para(doc, "", space_after=4)
    # rule
    hr = doc.add_paragraph(); hr.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(6)
    pPr = hr._p.get_or_add_pPr(); pbdr = OxmlElement("w:pBdr")
    bottom = OxmlElement("w:bottom")
    bottom.set(qn("w:val"),"single"); bottom.set(qn("w:sz"),"6")
    bottom.set(qn("w:space"),"1"); bottom.set(qn("w:color"),"auto")
    pbdr.append(bottom); pPr.append(pbdr)

    para(doc,
         "This memorandum flags threshold issues the team should resolve before or "
         "contemporaneously with filing the accompanying Rule 12(b)(6) motion to dismiss "
         "(\"MTD\"), proposed order, and this memo. The MTD is strong on the merits\u2014the "
         "research compilation supports dismissal of all five counts\u2014but several threshold "
         "matters warrant attention because they could affect the motion's viability, the "
         "forum, or the relief obtained. Issues are listed in approximate order of urgency.")

    # 1. Jurisdiction
    heading(doc, "1.  Subject-Matter Jurisdiction \u2014 A Likely Complete-Diversity Defect "
                 "(HIGHEST PRIORITY).", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The most significant threshold issue is a likely defect in the Court's diversity "
         "jurisdiction. The Notice of Removal alleges that \"Plaintiff Arcadia Health "
         "Systems, LLC is a citizen of the State of Texas,\" reasoning only from the "
         "domicile of Dr. Okonkwo as managing member. That analysis is incomplete. An LLC's "
         "citizenship is determined by tracing through all of its members, and a limited "
         "partnership's citizenship by tracing through all of its partners. Harvey v. Grey "
         "Wolf Drilling Co., 542 F.3d 1077, 1080 (5th Cir. 2008); Carden v. Arkoma Assocs., "
         "494 U.S. 185, 195\u201396 (1990).")
    para(doc,
         "The FAC itself (and Arcadia's Operating Agreement, produced in discovery) disclose "
         "Arcadia's full ownership chain. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 7\u20138. Arcadia's three members are: "
         "(1) Dr. Rachel Okonkwo (Texas); (2) Martin Schreiber (Texas); and (3) Apex Medical "
         "Ventures, LP, a Delaware limited partnership. Apex's partners are (a) Apex Medical "
         "Ventures GP, Inc.\u2014a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business in "
         "Wilmington, Delaware (a citizen of Delaware)\u2014and (b) the Okonkwo Family Trust "
         "(trustee and beneficiary domiciled in Texas). Arcadia is therefore a citizen of "
         "both Texas and Delaware. Meridian is a citizen of both Delaware (incorporation) "
         "and Texas (principal place of business, Austin). FAC \u00b6 9. Because both parties "
         "share citizenship in Delaware and Texas, complete diversity does not exist, and "
         "the Court likely lacks subject-matter jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C. \u00a7 1332(a). "
         "Subject-matter jurisdiction cannot be waived and may be raised sua sponte at any "
         "time. Arbaugh v. Y & H Corp., 546 U.S. 500, 514 (2006).")
    para(doc,
         "Strategic implications. This is a two-edged sword and requires a deliberate client "
         "decision:")
    para(doc,
         "(a)  If we do nothing, the Court (or Arcadia) may raise the defect at any time, "
         "potentially after we have invested in the MTD and discovery, and the case would be "
         "remanded to Bexar County state court\u2014the forum Arcadia originally chose.",
         indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "(b)  If we move to remand (or alert the Court), we surrender the federal forum. "
         "The federal forum has real advantages: Judge Alvarez's rigorous pleading standards "
         "(which favor our MTD), the bench trial (the \u00a7 12.9 jury waiver is being enforced), "
         "and the procedural predictability of federal court. State court in Bexar County is "
         "Arcadia's home court and may be less hospitable to our contract-based defenses.",
         indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "(c)  The trust-citizenship question deserves a closer look. The Operating Agreement "
         "describes the Okonkwo Family Trust as a revocable inter vivos trust with Dr. "
         "Okonkwo as sole trustee and primary beneficiary. Fifth Circuit law generally "
         "looks to the trustee's citizenship for a trust, but the analysis can turn on "
         "whether the trust resembles an unincorporated association. Even assuming the trust "
         "is a Texas citizen, the Apex GP's Delaware citizenship is alone sufficient to "
         "destroy diversity\u2014so the defect holds under any trust-citizenship theory.",
         indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "Recommendation. We should (i) confirm the citizenship analysis against the Apex "
         "partnership agreement (already requested) and any trust documents; (ii) present "
         "the client with a written forum-strategy decision (federal MTD vs. state-court "
         "remand) before filing; and (iii) regardless of the client's choice, preserve the "
         "objection\u2014the Answer already reserves \"all rights and defenses related to "
         "subject matter jurisdiction.\" If the client elects to proceed in federal court, "
         "we should be prepared for the Court to raise the issue sua sponte and to address "
         "it in a separate jurisdictional filing rather than burying it in the MTD.")

    # 2. Post-answer posture
    heading(doc, "2.  Post-Answer Posture \u2014 Rule 12(b)(6) vs. Rule 12(c).", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Meridian answered the FAC on October 2, 2023, before moving to dismiss. Rule "
         "12(h)(2) preserves a failure-to-state-a-claim defense raised after answering, and "
         "the Answer expressly preserved the Rule 12(b)(6) defense (First Affirmative "
         "Defense; Reservation of Rights). The Court's Standing Order \u00a7 3.4 permits "
         "post-answer Rule 12(b)(6) motions where the defense was preserved and treats them "
         "under the Rule 12(b)(6) standard. The Scheduling Order, however, states the Court "
         "will \"treat any such motion as a Rule 12(c) motion for judgment on the pleadings, "
         "evaluated under the same standard as a Rule 12(b)(6) motion.\"")
    para(doc,
         "The practical effect is nil\u2014the Twombly/Iqbal standard is identical\u2014but the "
         "caption and framing should anticipate the Court's characterization. The MTD is "
         "captioned as a Rule 12(b)(6) motion (consistent with the Standing Order and the "
         "task), and the legal-standard section notes the preserved defense. If the Court "
         "recharacterizes it as Rule 12(c), no prejudice arises because the standard and the "
         "documents reviewable (the FAC plus the MSLSA/SOW-1/Change Orders referenced in and "
         "central to it) are the same. One caution: under Rule 12(c), the Court may also "
         "consider the Answer; our Answer's affirmative allegations (e.g., the 45-day PM "
         "gap, the 75-day API-spec delay, Linden Park's migration errors) are consistent "
         "with\u2014not contrary to\u2014the MTD, so recharacterization does not hurt us.")

    # 3. Choice of law
    heading(doc, "3.  Choice of Law \u2014 Delaware (Contract) vs. Texas (Tort).", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Section 12.7 selects Delaware law for the contract, without regard to conflict-of-"
         "law principles. Delaware law therefore governs Count I and the unjust-enrichment "
         "analysis (both Delaware and Texas bar unjust enrichment where an express contract "
         "governs, so the result is the same). For the tort claims (Counts II\u2013III), the "
         "scope of \u00a7 12.7 is debatable: a broad reading extends Delaware law to all claims "
         "arising from the contractual relationship, while the most-significant-relationship "
         "test (all contacts point to Texas) likely brings Texas law to bear. The MTD argues "
         "the tort claims fail under either body of law\u2014Delaware's broader economic-loss "
         "doctrine and anti-reliance doctrine (Brasby, Kuhn, Eagle Industries) and Texas's "
         "duty-based economic-loss rule and puffery/reliance doctrine (Sharyland, Chapman, "
         "Pizza Hut, Presidio). We should be prepared for Arcadia to argue Texas law applies "
         "to the torts and to respond that the claims fail under Texas law regardless. The "
         "DTPA (Count V) is a Texas statute in any event, and the \u00a7 17.49(f) exemption is "
         "dispositive without regard to choice of law.")

    # 4. DTPA statutory text
    heading(doc, "4.  DTPA \u2014 Verify the \u00a7 17.49(f) Text and Confirm the $25M Asset "
                 "Alternative.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD leads with the \u00a7 17.49(f) exemption: a transaction involving total "
         "consideration by the consumer of more than $500,000, unless the consumer is an "
         "individual. Arcadia is an LLC and total consideration is $17,050,000\u2014a clean, "
         "dispositive, no-facts-needed ground. Two cautions:")
    para(doc,
         "(a)  Statutory-text verification. The research memo frames \u00a7 17.49(f) as the "
         "$500,000 consideration threshold (\"unless the consumer is an individual\"), while "
         "the Answer's Thirteenth Affirmative Defense references a $25 million asset test. "
         "The DTPA has been amended repeatedly. Before the response brief is filed, we "
         "should pull the current, official text of \u00a7 17.49(f) from the Texas Legislature's "
         "website and confirm the precise operative language and effective date, so the "
         "citation in the MTD and any reply is exact. The $500,000 consideration argument "
         "does not depend on the asset test, so it stands even if the asset language is "
         "narrower than the Answer assumed.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "(b)  The $25 million asset alternative. If the current statute also exempts "
         "transactions by a business consumer with assets of $25 million or more, that is an "
         "independent ground\u2014but it requires proof of Arcadia's total assets as of the "
         "transaction. The FAC does not state Arcadia's assets (it states $62 million in "
         "annual revenue and $30 million-plus in infrastructure investments, which strongly "
         "implies assets above $25 million). Our July 10, 2023 DTPA-response letter already "
         "demanded Arcadia's audited/reviewed financials. We should press that demand in "
         "discovery and, if the asset ground is needed, support it with Arcadia's balance "
         "sheet rather than inference.", indent=0.25)

    # 5. DTPA 60-day notice
    heading(doc, "5.  DTPA \u2014 Possible Noncompliance with the 60-Day Pre-Suit Notice "
                 "(\u00a7 17.505(a)).", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Section 17.505(a) requires a consumer to give written notice at least 60 days "
         "before filing suit. Arcadia's DTPA demand letter is dated June 15, 2023; the "
         "Original Petition was filed August 7, 2023\u2014only 53 days later, i.e., before the "
         "60-day period expired. (Our July 10, 2023 response letter refers to Arcadia's "
         "letter as dated June 26, 2023; if that is the operative date, the gap is only 42 "
         "days. Either way, the 60-day period was not satisfied.) This is a threshold "
         "defect, but it is not a clean Rule 12(b)(6) basis for dismissal: the remedy for "
         "noncompliance is abatement, not dismissal, see Tex. Bus. & Com. Code \u00a7 17.505(d), "
         "and it depends on dates outside the FAC. We therefore did not make it a primary "
         "MTD ground. Recommendation: preserve it as a defense/plea in abatement, and "
         "consider raising it if the DTPA count somehow survives the \u00a7 17.49(f) exemption. "
         "We should also reconcile the June 15 vs. June 26 date discrepancy in the record.")

    # 6. Damages cap
    heading(doc, "6.  Section 8.1 Liability Cap \u2014 Calculate the Exact Amount.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD's alternative argument caps all damages at the fees paid or payable in the "
         "twelve months preceding \"the event giving rise to such liability.\" Two "
         "open items: (a) the relevant 12-month period and the \"event\" that triggers it "
         "are ambiguous (the alleged pre-sale misrepresentations predate any fees; Go-Live "
         "was January 15, 2023; the first complaint was March 8, 2023); and (b) we need "
         "Meridian's billing records to compute the exact cap. The August 25, 2022 project "
         "status report shows $6.53 million invoiced to date as of that report, with "
         "milestone-based billing thereafter. We should obtain the complete invoice/payment "
         "ledger and compute the cap under each plausible trigger date so the alternative "
         "relief is precise if the Court reaches it.")

    # 7. Count I strategy
    heading(doc, "7.  Count I Strategy \u2014 Full Dismissal vs. Damages Limitation.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD seeks full dismissal of Count I (deemed acceptance, expired warranty, "
         "exclusive remedies, misattributed data-migration breaches) and, in the "
         "alternative, dismissal of all consequential/punitive damages and application of "
         "the \u00a7 8.1 cap. Full dismissal of a breach-of-contract claim at the pleading "
         "stage is aggressive; courts sometimes view deemed-acceptance and warranty-"
         "expiration as affirmative defenses better resolved on a more developed record. "
         "Simulados (W.D. Tex.) and Precision Healthcare (N.D. Tex.) support us, but the "
         "Court may prefer to limit damages rather than dismiss the count outright. The "
         "dual-track framing in the MTD hedges this risk. If the Court signals reluctance to "
         "dismiss Count I entirely at the hearing, we should be ready to press the "
         "alternative (which is very strong: the consequential-damages waiver alone "
         "eliminates roughly $30 million of the $47.3 million demand, and Arcadia's counsel "
         "expressly accepted that waiver \"knowingly and with the benefit of legal "
         "counsel\").")

    # 8. Jury waiver
    heading(doc, "8.  Jury-Trial Waiver (\u00a7 12.9) \u2014 Confirm Enforceability.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The Scheduling Order confirms the parties waived jury trial under \u00a7 12.9 and that "
         "the case will be tried to the bench. The FAC nonetheless demands a jury (FAC "
         "\u00b6 122), and our Answer conditionally demands a jury should the Court find the "
         "waiver unenforceable. This does not affect the MTD, but it affects trial strategy "
         "and the value of the federal forum. We should confirm there is no basis to "
         "challenge the waiver (it is mutual, negotiated, and expressly acknowledged in "
         "\u00a7 12.9), and be prepared to enforce it if the case proceeds. The negotiation "
         "record confirms Arcadia's counsel expressly agreed to the jury waiver.")

    # 9. Documents on the motion
    heading(doc, "9.  Documents the Court May Consider \u2014 Stay Within the Pleadings.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD relies on the FAC plus documents referenced in and central to it: the "
         "MSLSA, SOW-1, the four Change Orders, the December 9, 2021 proposal, the August "
         "25, 2022 project status report, and the March 8, 2023 email. Lormand permits "
         "consideration of these without conversion. We deliberately did not rely on the "
         "Schreiber due-diligence memo, the contract-negotiation email chain, or the support-"
         "ticket summary as primary MTD authority, because they are not referenced in the "
         "FAC and relying on them could trigger Rule 12(d) conversion. They remain powerful "
         "for summary judgment and for rebutting any Arcadia response that opens the door. "
         "If Arcadia's response introduces these materials (or argues reliance/due "
         "diligence), we can deploy them in the reply or move for partial summary judgment.")

    # 10. Page limits / filing
    heading(doc, "10.  Filing Mechanics.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD is within the Standing Order's 25-page limit for motions to dismiss "
         "(exclusive of the caption, table of authorities, signature block, and "
         "certificates). The proposed order is filed separately as a .docx, per Standing "
         "Order \u00a7 3.5; we will update the docket-entry number in the proposed order within "
         "one business day of filing. The certificate of conference is included in the MTD; "
         "if the Court prefers it as a separate docket entry, we will file it separately. "
         "The dispositive-motion deadline is January 15, 2024; the response is due 21 days "
         "later and the reply 14 days after that. We should calendar those dates and begin "
         "drafting the reply in anticipation of Arcadia's likely arguments (consumer status, "
         "fraudulent-inducement exception to the economic loss rule, and an attempt to "
         "distinguish Simulados).")

    para(doc, "", space_after=6)
    para(doc,
         "Please advise on the forum-strategy decision in Item 1 at your earliest "
         "convenience, as it may affect whether and how we file the MTD. We are available to "
         "discuss any of the foregoing.")

    save(doc, "cover-memo.docx")
    return doc

# ---- run ----
build_motion()
build_order()
build_memo()
print("ALL DONE")
PYEOF
echo "appended order + memo""}

         align="center", size=11, space_after=8)
    para(doc, "PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL  \u2022  ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT",
         bold=True, align="center", size=11, space_after=10)

    heading(doc, "MEMORANDUM", level=1, space_before=0)
    # memo header table
    hdr = [
        ("TO:", "Margaret \"Meg\" Calloway, Lead Partner; David Arsenault, Senior Associate"),
        ("FROM:", "Litigation Team, Stonebridge & Calloway LLP"),
        ("DATE:", "January 12, 2024"),
        ("RE:", "Threshold Issues \u2014 Filing of Rule 12(b)(6) Motion to Dismiss; "
                "Arcadia Health Systems, LLC v. Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc., "
                "No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA (W.D. Tex., Austin Div.)"),
    ]
    for label, val in hdr:
        p = doc.add_paragraph()
        p.paragraph_format.line_spacing_rule = WD_LINE_SPACING.SINGLE
        p.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(2)
        r1 = p.add_run(label + "  "); _set_run(r1, bold=True)
        r2 = p.add_run(val); _set_run(r2)
    para(doc, "", space_after=4)
    # rule
    hr = doc.add_paragraph(); hr.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt(6)
    pPr = hr._p.get_or_add_pPr(); pbdr = OxmlElement("w:pBdr")
    bottom = OxmlElement("w:bottom")
    bottom.set(qn("w:val"),"single"); bottom.set(qn("w:sz"),"6")
    bottom.set(qn("w:space"),"1"); bottom.set(qn("w:color"),"auto")
    pbdr.append(bottom); pPr.append(pbdr)

    para(doc,
         "This memorandum flags threshold issues the team should resolve before or "
         "contemporaneously with filing the accompanying Rule 12(b)(6) motion to dismiss "
         "(\"MTD\"), proposed order, and this memo. The MTD is strong on the merits\u2014the "
         "research compilation supports dismissal of all five counts\u2014but several threshold "
         "matters warrant attention because they could affect the motion's viability, the "
         "forum, or the relief obtained. Issues are listed in approximate order of urgency.")

    # 1. Jurisdiction
    heading(doc, "1.  Subject-Matter Jurisdiction \u2014 A Likely Complete-Diversity Defect "
                 "(HIGHEST PRIORITY).", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The most significant threshold issue is a likely defect in the Court's diversity "
         "jurisdiction. The Notice of Removal alleges that \"Plaintiff Arcadia Health "
         "Systems, LLC is a citizen of the State of Texas,\" reasoning only from the "
         "domicile of Dr. Okonkwo as managing member. That analysis is incomplete. An LLC's "
         "citizenship is determined by tracing through all of its members, and a limited "
         "partnership's citizenship by tracing through all of its partners. Harvey v. Grey "
         "Wolf Drilling Co., 542 F.3d 1077, 1080 (5th Cir. 2008); Carden v. Arkoma Assocs., "
         "494 U.S. 185, 195\u201396 (1990).")
    para(doc,
         "The FAC itself (and Arcadia's Operating Agreement, produced in discovery) disclose "
         "Arcadia's full ownership chain. FAC \u00b6\u00b6 7\u20138. Arcadia's three members are: "
         "(1) Dr. Rachel Okonkwo (Texas); (2) Martin Schreiber (Texas); and (3) Apex Medical "
         "Ventures, LP, a Delaware limited partnership. Apex's partners are (a) Apex Medical "
         "Ventures GP, Inc.\u2014a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business in "
         "Wilmington, Delaware (a citizen of Delaware)\u2014and (b) the Okonkwo Family Trust "
         "(trustee and beneficiary domiciled in Texas). Arcadia is therefore a citizen of "
         "both Texas and Delaware. Meridian is a citizen of both Delaware (incorporation) "
         "and Texas (principal place of business, Austin). FAC \u00b6 9. Because both parties "
         "share citizenship in Delaware and Texas, complete diversity does not exist, and "
         "the Court likely lacks subject-matter jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C. \u00a7 1332(a). "
         "Subject-matter jurisdiction cannot be waived and may be raised sua sponte at any "
         "time. Arbaugh v. Y & H Corp., 546 U.S. 500, 514 (2006).")
    para(doc,
         "Strategic implications. This is a two-edged sword and requires a deliberate client "
         "decision:")
    para(doc,
         "(a)  If we do nothing, the Court (or Arcadia) may raise the defect at any time, "
         "potentially after we have invested in the MTD and discovery, and the case would be "
         "remanded to Bexar County state court\u2014the forum Arcadia originally chose.",
         indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "(b)  If we move to remand (or alert the Court), we surrender the federal forum. "
         "The federal forum has real advantages: Judge Alvarez's rigorous pleading standards "
         "(which favor our MTD), the bench trial (the \u00a7 12.9 jury waiver is being enforced), "
         "and the procedural predictability of federal court. State court in Bexar County is "
         "Arcadia's home court and may be less hospitable to our contract-based defenses.",
         indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "(c)  The trust-citizenship question deserves a closer look. The Operating Agreement "
         "describes the Okonkwo Family Trust as a revocable inter vivos trust with Dr. "
         "Okonkwo as sole trustee and primary beneficiary. Fifth Circuit law generally "
         "looks to the trustee's citizenship for a trust, but the analysis can turn on "
         "whether the trust resembles an unincorporated association. Even assuming the trust "
         "is a Texas citizen, the Apex GP's Delaware citizenship is alone sufficient to "
         "destroy diversity\u2014so the defect holds under any trust-citizenship theory.",
         indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "Recommendation. We should (i) confirm the citizenship analysis against the Apex "
         "partnership agreement (already requested) and any trust documents; (ii) present "
         "the client with a written forum-strategy decision (federal MTD vs. state-court "
         "remand) before filing; and (iii) regardless of the client's choice, preserve the "
         "objection\u2014the Answer already reserves \"all rights and defenses related to "
         "subject matter jurisdiction.\" If the client elects to proceed in federal court, "
         "we should be prepared for the Court to raise the issue sua sponte and to address "
         "it in a separate jurisdictional filing rather than burying it in the MTD.")

    # 2. Post-answer posture
    heading(doc, "2.  Post-Answer Posture \u2014 Rule 12(b)(6) vs. Rule 12(c).", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Meridian answered the FAC on October 2, 2023, before moving to dismiss. Rule "
         "12(h)(2) preserves a failure-to-state-a-claim defense raised after answering, and "
         "the Answer expressly preserved the Rule 12(b)(6) defense (First Affirmative "
         "Defense; Reservation of Rights). The Court's Standing Order \u00a7 3.4 permits "
         "post-answer Rule 12(b)(6) motions where the defense was preserved and treats them "
         "under the Rule 12(b)(6) standard. The Scheduling Order, however, states the Court "
         "will \"treat any such motion as a Rule 12(c) motion for judgment on the pleadings, "
         "evaluated under the same standard as a Rule 12(b)(6) motion.\"")
    para(doc,
         "The practical effect is nil\u2014the Twombly/Iqbal standard is identical\u2014but the "
         "caption and framing should anticipate the Court's characterization. The MTD is "
         "captioned as a Rule 12(b)(6) motion (consistent with the Standing Order and the "
         "task), and the legal-standard section notes the preserved defense. If the Court "
         "recharacterizes it as Rule 12(c), no prejudice arises because the standard and the "
         "documents reviewable (the FAC plus the MSLSA/SOW-1/Change Orders referenced in and "
         "central to it) are the same. One caution: under Rule 12(c), the Court may also "
         "consider the Answer; our Answer's affirmative allegations (e.g., the 45-day PM "
         "gap, the 75-day API-spec delay, Linden Park's migration errors) are consistent "
         "with\u2014not contrary to\u2014the MTD, so recharacterization does not hurt us.")

    # 3. Choice of law
    heading(doc, "3.  Choice of Law \u2014 Delaware (Contract) vs. Texas (Tort).", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Section 12.7 selects Delaware law for the contract, without regard to conflict-of-"
         "law principles. Delaware law therefore governs Count I and the unjust-enrichment "
         "analysis (both Delaware and Texas bar unjust enrichment where an express contract "
         "governs, so the result is the same). For the tort claims (Counts II\u2013III), the "
         "scope of \u00a7 12.7 is debatable: a broad reading extends Delaware law to all claims "
         "arising from the contractual relationship, while the most-significant-relationship "
         "test (all contacts point to Texas) likely brings Texas law to bear. The MTD argues "
         "the tort claims fail under either body of law\u2014Delaware's broader economic-loss "
         "doctrine and anti-reliance doctrine (Brasby, Kuhn, Eagle Industries) and Texas's "
         "duty-based economic-loss rule and puffery/reliance doctrine (Sharyland, Chapman, "
         "Pizza Hut, Presidio). We should be prepared for Arcadia to argue Texas law applies "
         "to the torts and to respond that the claims fail under Texas law regardless. The "
         "DTPA (Count V) is a Texas statute in any event, and the \u00a7 17.49(f) exemption is "
         "dispositive without regard to choice of law.")

    # 4. DTPA statutory text
    heading(doc, "4.  DTPA \u2014 Verify the \u00a7 17.49(f) Text and Confirm the $25M Asset "
                 "Alternative.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD leads with the \u00a7 17.49(f) exemption: a transaction involving total "
         "consideration by the consumer of more than $500,000, unless the consumer is an "
         "individual. Arcadia is an LLC and total consideration is $17,050,000\u2014a clean, "
         "dispositive, no-facts-needed ground. Two cautions:")
    para(doc,
         "(a)  Statutory-text verification. The research memo frames \u00a7 17.49(f) as the "
         "$500,000 consideration threshold (\"unless the consumer is an individual\"), while "
         "the Answer's Thirteenth Affirmative Defense references a $25 million asset test. "
         "The DTPA has been amended repeatedly. Before the response brief is filed, we "
         "should pull the current, official text of \u00a7 17.49(f) from the Texas Legislature's "
         "website and confirm the precise operative language and effective date, so the "
         "citation in the MTD and any reply is exact. The $500,000 consideration argument "
         "does not depend on the asset test, so it stands even if the asset language is "
         "narrower than the Answer assumed.", indent=0.25)
    para(doc,
         "(b)  The $25 million asset alternative. If the current statute also exempts "
         "transactions by a business consumer with assets of $25 million or more, that is an "
         "independent ground\u2014but it requires proof of Arcadia's total assets as of the "
         "transaction. The FAC does not state Arcadia's assets (it states $62 million in "
         "annual revenue and $30 million-plus in infrastructure investments, which strongly "
         "implies assets above $25 million). Our July 10, 2023 DTPA-response letter already "
         "demanded Arcadia's audited/reviewed financials. We should press that demand in "
         "discovery and, if the asset ground is needed, support it with Arcadia's balance "
         "sheet rather than inference.", indent=0.25)

    # 5. DTPA 60-day notice
    heading(doc, "5.  DTPA \u2014 Possible Noncompliance with the 60-Day Pre-Suit Notice "
                 "(\u00a7 17.505(a)).", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "Section 17.505(a) requires a consumer to give written notice at least 60 days "
         "before filing suit. Arcadia's DTPA demand letter is dated June 15, 2023; the "
         "Original Petition was filed August 7, 2023\u2014only 53 days later, i.e., before the "
         "60-day period expired. (Our July 10, 2023 response letter refers to Arcadia's "
         "letter as dated June 26, 2023; if that is the operative date, the gap is only 42 "
         "days. Either way, the 60-day period was not satisfied.) This is a threshold "
         "defect, but it is not a clean Rule 12(b)(6) basis for dismissal: the remedy for "
         "noncompliance is abatement, not dismissal, see Tex. Bus. & Com. Code \u00a7 17.505(d), "
         "and it depends on dates outside the FAC. We therefore did not make it a primary "
         "MTD ground. Recommendation: preserve it as a defense/plea in abatement, and "
         "consider raising it if the DTPA count somehow survives the \u00a7 17.49(f) exemption. "
         "We should also reconcile the June 15 vs. June 26 date discrepancy in the record.")

    # 6. Damages cap
    heading(doc, "6.  Section 8.1 Liability Cap \u2014 Calculate the Exact Amount.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD's alternative argument caps all damages at the fees paid or payable in the "
         "twelve months preceding \"the event giving rise to such liability.\" Two "
         "open items: (a) the relevant 12-month period and the \"event\" that triggers it "
         "are ambiguous (the alleged pre-sale misrepresentations predate any fees; Go-Live "
         "was January 15, 2023; the first complaint was March 8, 2023); and (b) we need "
         "Meridian's billing records to compute the exact cap. The August 25, 2022 project "
         "status report shows $6.53 million invoiced to date as of that report, with "
         "milestone-based billing thereafter. We should obtain the complete invoice/payment "
         "ledger and compute the cap under each plausible trigger date so the alternative "
         "relief is precise if the Court reaches it.")

    # 7. Count I strategy
    heading(doc, "7.  Count I Strategy \u2014 Full Dismissal vs. Damages Limitation.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD seeks full dismissal of Count I (deemed acceptance, expired warranty, "
         "exclusive remedies, misattributed data-migration breaches) and, in the "
         "alternative, dismissal of all consequential/punitive damages and application of "
         "the \u00a7 8.1 cap. Full dismissal of a breach-of-contract claim at the pleading "
         "stage is aggressive; courts sometimes view deemed-acceptance and warranty-"
         "expiration as affirmative defenses better resolved on a more developed record. "
         "Simulados (W.D. Tex.) and Precision Healthcare (N.D. Tex.) support us, but the "
         "Court may prefer to limit damages rather than dismiss the count outright. The "
         "dual-track framing in the MTD hedges this risk. If the Court signals reluctance to "
         "dismiss Count I entirely at the hearing, we should be ready to press the "
         "alternative (which is very strong: the consequential-damages waiver alone "
         "eliminates roughly $30 million of the $47.3 million demand, and Arcadia's counsel "
         "expressly accepted that waiver \"knowingly and with the benefit of legal "
         "counsel\").")

    # 8. Jury waiver
    heading(doc, "8.  Jury-Trial Waiver (\u00a7 12.9) \u2014 Confirm Enforceability.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The Scheduling Order confirms the parties waived jury trial under \u00a7 12.9 and that "
         "the case will be tried to the bench. The FAC nonetheless demands a jury (FAC "
         "\u00b6 122), and our Answer conditionally demands a jury should the Court find the "
         "waiver unenforceable. This does not affect the MTD, but it affects trial strategy "
         "and the value of the federal forum. We should confirm there is no basis to "
         "challenge the waiver (it is mutual, negotiated, and expressly acknowledged in "
         "\u00a7 12.9), and be prepared to enforce it if the case proceeds. The negotiation "
         "record confirms Arcadia's counsel expressly agreed to the jury waiver.")

    # 9. Documents on the motion
    heading(doc, "9.  Documents the Court May Consider \u2014 Stay Within the Pleadings.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD relies on the FAC plus documents referenced in and central to it: the "
         "MSLSA, SOW-1, the four Change Orders, the December 9, 2021 proposal, the August "
         "25, 2022 project status report, and the March 8, 2023 email. Lormand permits "
         "consideration of these without conversion. We deliberately did not rely on the "
         "Schreiber due-diligence memo, the contract-negotiation email chain, or the support-"
         "ticket summary as primary MTD authority, because they are not referenced in the "
         "FAC and relying on them could trigger Rule 12(d) conversion. They remain powerful "
         "for summary judgment and for rebutting any Arcadia response that opens the door. "
         "If Arcadia's response introduces these materials (or argues reliance/due "
         "diligence), we can deploy them in the reply or move for partial summary judgment.")

    # 10. Page limits / filing
    heading(doc, "10.  Filing Mechanics.", level=2)
    para(doc,
         "The MTD is within the Standing Order's 25-page limit for motions to dismiss "
         "(exclusive of the caption, table of authorities, signature block, and "
         "certificates). The proposed order is filed separately as a .docx, per Standing "
         "Order \u00a7 3.5; we will update the docket-entry number in the proposed order within "
         "one business day of filing. The certificate of conference is included in the MTD; "
         "if the Court prefers it as a separate docket entry, we will file it separately. "
         "The dispositive-motion deadline is January 15, 2024; the response is due 21 days "
         "later and the reply 14 days after that. We should calendar those dates and begin "
         "drafting the reply in anticipation of Arcadia's likely arguments (consumer status, "
         "fraudulent-inducement exception to the economic loss rule, and an attempt to "
         "distinguish Simulados).")

    para(doc, "", space_after=6)
    para(doc,
         "Please advise on the forum-strategy decision in Item 1 at your earliest "
         "convenience, as it may affect whether and how we file the MTD. We are available to "
         "discuss any of the foregoing.")

    save(doc, "cover-memo.docx")
    return doc

UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT

                     WESTERN DISTRICT OF TEXAS

                            AUSTIN DIVISION

ARCADIA HEALTH SYSTEMS, LLC,            Civil Action No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA
Plaintiff,
v.
MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS,
INC.,
Defendant.


 DEFENDANT MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION TO

 DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT PURSUANT

         TO FEDERAL RULE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 12(B)(6)

         Response Due: Twenty-one (21) days after filing of this Motion




CERTIFICATE OF CONFERENCE

Pursuant to Section 3.1 of the Court's Standing Order for Civil Cases and

Local Rule CV-7, counsel for Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.

("Meridian") conferred in good faith with counsel for Plaintiff Arcadia

Health Systems, LLC ("Arcadia") regarding the relief sought in this Motion.

The parties discussed the legal grounds raised herein, including the

statutory exemption applicable to Arcadia's DTPA claim, the bar on unjust

enrichment in the presence of an express contract, and the contractual and

pleading deficiencies affecting Arcadia's tort and contract claims. Arcadia

declined to voluntarily dismiss or narrow any claim. Because this Motion


                                       1
 presents legal questions on which no compromise is possible, Meridian

submits that further conferral would be futile and respectfully files this

Motion.

I. INTRODUCTION

This is a breach-of-contract case dressed up as something more. Arcadia

Health Systems, LLC—a sophisticated commercial entity with approximately

$62 million in annual revenue, represented by experienced outside counsel

throughout an arm's-length negotiation—paid $14.7 million (plus $2.35

million in mutually executed Change Orders) to license and implement

Meridian's NexusCore healthcare analytics platform. Dissatisfied with the

result, Arcadia now seeks $47.3 million by repackaging a single commercial

dispute into five causes of action and asking the Court to disregard the

comprehensive risk-allocation framework that Arcadia's own counsel

negotiated and Arcadia knowingly accepted.

========== PROPOSED ORDER ==========
                   UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT

                     WESTERN DISTRICT OF TEXAS

                              AUSTIN DIVISION

ARCADIA HEALTH SYSTEMS, LLC,           Civil Action No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA
Plaintiff,
v.
MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS,
INC.,
Defendant.


                                     §1

ORDER

On this day came on to be considered Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions,

Inc.'s Motion to Dismiss Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint Pursuant to

Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 12(b)(6) (Dkt. No. [___]), and the Court,

having reviewed the Motion, the First Amended Complaint, the governing

Master Software License and Services Agreement and related documents,

and the applicable law, is of the opinion that the Motion should be granted.

IT IS THEREFORE ORDERED, ADJUDGED, AND DECREED that

Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.'s Motion to Dismiss (Dkt.

No. [___]) is GRANTED.

IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that all five counts of Plaintiff Arcadia Health

Systems, LLC's First Amended Complaint are DISMISSED WITH

PREJUDICE, namely:

      (1) Count I — Breach of Contract;

      (2) Count II — Fraud;


                                      1
         (3) Count III — Negligent Misrepresentation;

        (4) Count IV — Unjust Enrichment; and

        (5) Count V — Violation of the Texas Deceptive Trade Practices–

        Consumer Protection Act, Tex. Bus. & Com. Code §§ 17.41–17.63.

IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that, in the event the Court does not dismiss

Count I in its entirety, all of Plaintiff's claims for consequential, incidental,

special, punitive, and exemplary damages—including claims for lost profits,

increased operating costs, reputational harm, and treble damages—are

DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE as barred by Section 8.2 of the Master

Software License and Services Agreement, and all remaining damages are

limited to the aggregate-liability cap set forth in Section 8.1 of that

Agreement.

IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Plaintiff Arcadia Health Systems, LLC shall

take nothing by its First Amended Complaint, that all claims against

Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. are DISMISSED WITH

PREJUDICE, and that Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. be go hence

without day and recover its costs of court, for which execution may issue,

together with such other and further relief to which Defendant may be

entitled under the Master Software License and Services Agreement or

applicable law.

IT IS FINALLY ORDERED that this Order is final and appealable as to all

claims and all parties, and that the Clerk of Court is directed to close this

case.



                                         2
 SIGNED on this _____ day of ____________, 2024.



_______________________________________

THE HONORABLE CATHERINE M. ALVAREZ

United States District Judge

Western District of Texas

Austin Division




                                      3
 
========== COVER MEMO (first 60 lines) ==========
                    STONEBRIDGE & CALLOWAY LLP

            600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2800 • Austin, Texas 78701

             Telephone (512) 555-4200 • Facsimile (512) 555-4201


     PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL • ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT


MEMORANDUM

TO: Margaret "Meg" Calloway, Lead Partner; David Arsenault, Senior
Associate
FROM: Litigation Team, Stonebridge & Calloway LLP
DATE: January 12, 2024
RE: Threshold Issues — Filing of Rule 12(b)(6) Motion to Dismiss; Arcadia
Health Systems, LLC v. Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc., No. 1:23-cv-00847-
CMA (W.D. Tex., Austin Div.)




This memorandum flags threshold issues the team should resolve before or

contemporaneously with filing the accompanying Rule 12(b)(6) motion to

dismiss ("MTD"), proposed order, and this memo. The MTD is strong on the

merits—the research compilation supports dismissal of all five counts—but

several threshold matters warrant attention because they could affect the

motion's viability, the forum, or the relief obtained. Issues are listed in

approximate order of urgency.

1. Subject-Matter Jurisdiction — A Likely Complete-Diversity Defect

(HIGHEST PRIORITY).

The most significant threshold issue is a likely defect in the Court's diversity

jurisdiction. The Notice of Removal alleges that "Plaintiff Arcadia Health



                                        1
 Systems, LLC is a citizen of the State of Texas," reasoning only from the

domicile of Dr. Okonkwo as managing member. That analysis is incomplete.

An LLC's citizenship is determined by tracing through all of its members,

and a limited partnership's citizenship by tracing through all of its partners.

Harvey v. Grey Wolf Drilling Co., 542 F.3d 1077, 1080 (5th Cir. 2008);

Carden v. Arkoma Assocs., 494 U.S. 185, 195–96 (1990).

tool_calls.arguments:
{"file_path": "response.md", "content": "# Deliverables — *Arcadia Health Systems, LLC v. Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.*, No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA (W.D. Tex.)

Three Word documents produced in `output/`, each formatted to Judge Alvarez's Standing Order (12-pt Times New Roman, double-spaced, 1-inch margins, centered page numbers) and validated against the ECMA-376 schema:

| File | Pages | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `motion-to-dismiss.docx` | 23 (≈21 substantive + TOA) | Rule 12(b)(6) motion to dismiss all five counts |
| `proposed-order.docx` | 3 | Self-executing proposed order granting dismissal |
| `cover-memo.docx` | 10 | Privileged internal memo flagging threshold issues |

## Scope and method
Read the operative First Amended Complaint in full, then cross-referenced every supporting document: the MSLSA and SOW-1 (the governing contract), the four Change Orders, the defense research/case-law memo, Judge Alvarez's Standing Order, the scheduling order, Meridian's answer, the notice of removal, Arcadia's operating agreement, Schreiber's due-diligence memo, the Dec. 9, 2021 proposal, the March 8, 2023 email, the Aug. 25, 2022 project status report, the support-ticket summary, the DTPA pre-suit demand/response letters, and the contract-negotiation email chain. Issues were developed by reading each document against the others — most of the strongest arguments live in the *gaps* between the FAC's allegations and the contract/record.

## Motion to dismiss — argument map (all five counts)
- **Count V (DTPA)** — lead with the dispositive statutory exemption: Tex. Bus. & Com. Code § 17.49(f) exempts transactions with total consideration > $500,000 unless the consumer is an individual. Arcadia is an LLC; total consideration is $17,050,000. Alternatives: not a "consumer" (*Riverside Nat'l Bank*); puffery; no producing cause (*Melody Home*).
- **Count IV (Unjust Enrichment)** — barred because an express contract (the MSLSA) governs the same subject matter (*Fortune Production*; *Excess Underwriters*). Arcadia sues *on* the contract in Count I and never alleges it is void.
- **Count II (Fraud)** — four independent grounds: (1) Rule 9(b) particularity failure — post-hoc reasoning, no "why," conclusory "on information and belief" concealment (*Benchmark*; *Flaherty*); (2) economic loss rule under both Delaware (*Brasby*; *Kuhn*) and Texas (*Sharyland*; *Chapman*) law; (3) puffery (*Pizza Hut*; *Presidio*; *Castrol*) plus the proposal's express disclaimers; (4) the § 12.1 integration/non-reliance clause bars reliance on extra-contractual statements (*Eagle Industries*; *SIGA*; *Kuhn*), and reliance was not justifiable given Arcadia's own due diligence and counsel.
- **Count III (Negligent Misrepresentation)** — no independent duty (*McCamish*); economic loss rule; benefit-of-the-bargain damages not recoverable in tort (*Sloane*); Rule 9(b) applies to claims sounding in fraud; puffery/no justifiable reliance.
- **Count I (Breach of Contract)** — Delaware law governs (§ 12.7). Primary: deemed acceptance under § 5.3 (Go-Live Jan. 15, 2023 → deadline Feb. 14, 2023; March 8 email was 22 days late) (*Simulados*, W.D. Tex.; *Precision Healthcare*); expired 90-day warranty with exclusive remedy (§ 9.1); data-migration breaches misattributed (SOW-1 § 3.2 allocates them to Arcadia/Linden Park). Alternative: consequential/punitive damages barred by § 8.2 and all damages capped by § 8.1 (*Dresser-Rand*; *Kana Software*).

## Cover memo — threshold issues flagged (in priority order)
1. **Subject-matter jurisdiction / complete-diversity defect (highest priority).** The Notice of Removal treated Arcadia as a citizen of only Texas, but the FAC (¶¶ 7–8) and Arcadia's operating agreement show Arcadia is also a citizen of **Delaware** — through Apex Medical Ventures, LP, whose general partner (Apex Medical Ventures GP, Inc.) is a Delaware corporation. Meridian is a citizen of both Delaware and Texas. Complete diversity is destroyed; the Court likely lacks § 1332(a) jurisdiction (non-waivable, *Arbaugh*). This may moot the MTD and requires a deliberate client forum-strategy decision (federal MTD vs. state-court remand).
2. Post-answer posture — Rule 12(b)(6) vs. 12(c) (defense preserved in the Answer; same standard).
3. Choice of law — Delaware (contract) vs. Texas (tort); MTD argues the tort claims fail under either.
4. DTPA — verify the exact current text of § 17.49(f); confirm the $25M-asset alternative with Arcadia's financials.
5. DTPA — possible noncompliance with the 60-day pre-suit notice (§ 17.505(a)): demand dated June 15, 2023; suit filed Aug. 7, 2023 (53 days). Remedy is abatement, not dismissal, so not a primary MTD ground.
6. § 8.1 liability cap — compute the exact amount from Meridian's billing records.
7. Count I strategy — full dismissal vs. damages limitation (dual-track framing hedges the risk).
8. Jury-trial waiver (§ 12.9) — confirm enforceability (affects trial/forum value).
9. Documents reviewable on the motion — kept within the FAC + documents referenced in and central to it (*Lormand*); avoided reliance on the Schreiber memo/negotiation emails/support-ticket report as primary authority to prevent Rule 12(d) conversion.
10. Filing mechanics — page limits, separate proposed order, certificate of conference, briefing schedule.

## Notes / data gaps surfaced (not invented)
- The § 17.49(f) statutory text is framed as the research memo states ($500,000 consideration threshold); the memo flags that the precise current text and the $25M-asset alternative should be verified against the live statute and Arcadia's balance sheet.
- The DTPA demand-letter date is June 15, 2023 in the letter itself but June 26, 2023 in Meridian's response letter — a record discrepancy the memo flags for reconciliation.
- The § 8.1 cap amount cannot be fixed without Meridian's invoice/payment ledger — flagged as an open item rather than estimated."}

text:
All three deliverables are complete, schema-valid, and formatted to Judge Alvarez's Standing Order. Here's a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverables (in `output/`)

**`motion-to-dismiss.docx`** — A 23-page Rule 12(b)(6) motion to dismiss all five counts of the First Amended Complaint, with a table of authorities. It leads with the cleanest, dispositive arguments and addresses every count:

- **Count V (DTPA)** — dismissed under the Tex. Bus. & Com. Code § 17.49(f) exemption (total consideration $17.05M > $500,000; Arcadia is an LLC, not an individual), with alternative "not a consumer," puffery, and no-producing-cause grounds.
- **Count IV (Unjust Enrichment)** — barred because the express MSLSA governs the same subject matter (*Fortune Production*; *Excess Underwriters*).
- **Count II (Fraud)** — four independent grounds: Rule 9(b) failure, the economic loss rule (Delaware *and* Texas), puffery, and the § 12.1 integration/non-reliance clause plus no justifiable reliance.
- **Count III (Negligent Misrepresentation)** — no independent duty (*McCamish*), economic loss rule, benefit-of-the-bargain damages (*Sloane*), and Rule 9(b).
- **Count I (Breach of Contract)** — deemed acceptance under § 5.3 (the March 8 email was 22 days late), expired exclusive-remedy warranty, misattributed data-migration breaches, and alternatively a § 8.2 consequential-damages bar plus the § 8.1 liability cap.

**`proposed-order.docx`** — A self-executing 3-page proposed order dismissing all counts with prejudice (with the alternative damages-limitation track), signature line for Judge Alvarez.

**`cover-memo.docx`** — A privileged 10-page internal memo flagging ten threshold issues, led by the **highest-priority** one: a likely complete-diversity defect. The Notice of Removal treated Arcadia as a Texas-only citizen, but the FAC (¶¶ 7–8) and Arcadia's operating agreement show Arcadia is also a citizen of **Delaware** (through Apex Medical Ventures GP, Inc.) — the same states as Meridian — destroying § 1332(a) jurisdiction. The memo also covers the post-answer 12(b)(6)/12(c) posture, choice of law, § 17.49(f) text verification, the 60-day DTPA pre-suit-notice shortfall, the § 8.1 cap calculation, Count I strategy, the jury waiver, the documents reviewable on the motion, and filing mechanics.

## Method
I read the operative complaint in full and cross-referenced every supporting document — the MSLSA/SOW-1, the four Change Orders, the defense case-law memo, the standing and scheduling orders, Meridian's answer, the notice of removal, Arcadia's operating agreement, Schreiber's due-diligence memo, the Dec. 9 proposal disclaimers, the March 8 email, the project status report, the support-ticket summary, the DTPA demand correspondence, and the negotiation emails. The strongest arguments come from the gaps between the FAC's allegations and the contract/record (e.g., Arcadia's own counsel withdrew a fraud carve-out to the integration clause and accepted the consequential-damages waiver "knowingly and with the benefit of legal counsel"). All three files pass ECMA-376 schema validation.
